Content: What are you looking at?
Ah, there you are. Nothing really. I've just been wondering... if I wanted to land another astronomical investment to study all the Exoswarm coming off this giant mech...
What kind of pitch would it take to make every major player in Solaris nod along and funnel Credits into my pocket, year after year?
Have you figured it out?
Not yet. My conscience won't let me think that far. Luckily, the man who can figure it out is back.
...The old man who promised me the blade? The head of the Spacetrek Collective?

The very same, but don't let the quick agreement fool you. He's already thinking ten steps ahead. If Ashinohara wasn't in such dire straits, he wouldn't have agreed to forge that blade for you so easily.
Or at the very least, he'd have made sure he got his pound of flesh in return.
Take two. Give yourself a day to look around.
Go on. A Black Shores member will meet you there. Hmph... I still have all of Dr. Luuk's mess to clean up.
Understood. I am at your call should you require it.
Yes, I'm listening...
So it's about Denia... I see. Let's discuss it in person.
...I couldn't find the child. Time and space in that dimension are too fractured. My familiar lost its way.
Hm... then we need something that can hold out longer. A better way to search?
Voidmatter reactivity has been high lately. A clear sign to follow would be best.
That child is the Exostrider's Resonator. There's no clearer sign than that.
Looks like it's time the Exostrider was reactivated. When that happens, whether you're an annoying adult or the one meant to pilot it, I'm sure everyone will be on board, don't you think?
Ooh, what if we used footage of the Exostrider activating to pitch for funding?
And the confidentiality agreements?
No sympathy for my chronic funding anxiety?
Fine. Go to Honami. I already approved your leave of absence.
Why?
To... test the blade?
Even once the Exostrider awakens, there's still a chance it fails. If it does, what you sealed in the <te href=851075>Dimmr Plains</te> becomes a liability...
But it could also be our ace in the hole.
So before I give you the thankless task of guarding those plains, go deal with matters of the heart first.
...Understood. I will return in one day.

Hiyuki arrives at the frostlands to inform Lucilla that her origami familiar failed to locate Aemeath. Lucilla assigns Hiyuki the new task of guarding the Dimmr Plains while they carry on with their plan to awaken the Exostrider, a contingency if the plan fails. To help Hiyuki fulfill that duty with a clear mind, Lucilla suggests she first return to Honami City to deal with certain matters of the heart.
Hello, Lady Miko. I've been waiting for you for a very long time.
My final wish… was simply to see you one last time.
...Your final wish?
Yes. It's alright. I know I'm dead. I know I'm what they call a "Left Behind" now.
But I thought if I'm going to remain like this anyway, I may as well get what I wanted before I disappear, right?
I've heard of cases where the Left Behind remain aware of their state, sustained by powerful desires… But for such a simple wish…
You truly only wished to see me again?
Hehe. I mean, you are a legend. You've been the Miko of Flaming Sakura since my great-grandmother's time.
When I heard Honami was in danger and that the Miko of Flaming Sakura would meet the vanguard, I rushed here just to see you.
You shouldn't have taken such a risk. If you wished to see me, you could have gone to the Flaming Sakura.
I know, I know. Everyone says you can just write your wish on the plaque and Lady Miko will see it… but I worried. What if it never reached you?
So I came all this way to make the wish in person. I wanted to see whether it would come true.
Very well. Then tell me, what is your true wish?
I wish for world peace. For people to stop living in fear of the Lament. I want everyone to survive it—
Lady Miko, my wish is... "That there will never be another Miko of Flaming Sakura."
I see. Then I promise you. This wish will be fulfilled.
I know it will.
After all, you are the Eternal Miko—
Now, now. No need for that, Lady Miko.
If you wished, you could cut down this avatar at any moment. But… I think you'll find this far more interesting.
Gyokuro's <ano=white bird>Shiratori</ano>.
I know the Fractsidus has caused Ashinohara its fair share of grief. But personally? I hold no ill will toward you.
I had dealings with the previous miko. It wasn't exactly a pleasant chat, but I promise you, her death was not by my hand.
Just as you once witnessed, she met the fate all Mikos of Flaming Sakura share. She died burning her future to ash to pay for the present.
Aren't you even a little curious? About what I showed her?
If this makes you uncomfortable, feel free to cut through space and leave. This face of mine, of course, is entirely at your mercy.
No need to dull my blade.
Hmph... She wears the exact same expression as the previous miko. I suppose one day, she too will be reduced to ashes by her own wish.
Oh, Eternal Miko... is she simply arrogant? Or just a coward?

The Grand Architect, disguised as a lively Left Behind, mentions her wish "That there will never be another Miko of Flaming Sakura" to Hiyuki before dropping the act. 
In the confrontation, the Grand Architect reveals how Gyokuro burned through all her futures on the path to becoming miko, and that she herself had made contact with Gyokuro in the past. To uncover the truth, Hiyuki steps through a portal.
(A peaceful Honami... Why bother with such an illusion?)
Ugh, I'm late for school again...
Hey, think I can take tomorrow off? If work is the root of all of your problems, why work in the first place?
Come on. If work is your biggest problem, then you're probably already living a pretty good life.
(If Ashinohara could truly be this peaceful, if their dreams could really come true… then I wouldn't have to raise my blade ever again…)
Superhero movies these days are so boring. They should've just blown up all of Solaris! That would've been a real movie.
Man, just being able to have a normal, peaceful day like this is all we need.
Hmm, guess I'll go draw a fortune stick. Hopefully, I'll get lucky with men soon.
Gyokuro...?
Lady Gyokuro, guess what? No math homework today!
My parents and my big sister finally taught me how to make red bean pudding. Want to try mine?
I'd love to! You know I can't resist red bean pudding. Wouldn't miss your special batch for the world, little Kanna.
Yay! I'll go make it right now—
<ano=Older sister>Aneue</ano>, I'm here. What is it?
My, my. Can't a sister just want to see her favorite girl without a reason?
...That's not what I meant.
Hehe. There's that serious little face. I just wanted to chat. Always relaxes me.
So? How are things at the Flaming Sakura?
The usual. Many came to make wishes.
The Threnodian's active cycle is almost here. Death will be a frequent visitor to Ashinohara once more.
Prayers for safety, peace, victory… they pile up thickest at times like this.
The Miko of Flaming Sakura is the bridge between people and the divine. A "wish jar" for all their hopes. We must listen to every single one.
...Lately, I've been hearing you ring the <ano=ritual bell>Suzu</ano>.
To grant those wishes, you burn your future using the Suzu's power. Continue... and one day there'll be nothing left to burn.
You should have stepped down already, <ano=older sister>Aneue</ano>. Let me become the Miko of Flaming Sakura. Let me inherit the Suzu.
Oh, I'm perfectly fine! I've got futures to spare, you know?
You tell me, little Hiyuki. What's got you so fixated on this? Don't tell me you're secretly in awe watching my heroics on the battlefield, and—
Yes. I want to be just like you.
Heh... you wish! You're a hundred years too early for that, you little blockhead.
Enough about the Suzu! Stay and have some pudding with me instead. I know a wonderful little shop.
Always practicing archery under that sakura tree. What a bore! A girl your age should be out living a little!
...Here we go again. You know I don't care about any of that.
I'm going back.
You sure? Come on, consider granting your big sister's wish.
I only came because you called. I put my work on hold. I can't stay too long.
Besides... I don't like red bean pudding.
Huh?! I keep forgetting you have the world's most boring palate. What a shame.
I'll just have to enjoy it all by my lonesome, then!
Gyokuro never once spoke of her own wishes. She lived only to grant the wishes of others.
If only... I had chosen to go with her.

An illusory Honami lies on the other side of the portal. Hiyuki is shown past conversations between Gyokuro and herself. Back then, Hiyuki was timid and reserved, while Gyokuro always embraced her with a smile. Focused on the duty of inheriting her older sister's role as miko and the ability to wield the "Suzu," the past Hiyuki rejected Gyokuro's wish to share red bean pudding with her and returned home alone.
Now, regret lingers in Hiyuki's heart: If only she had chosen to stay with her sister.
(...I should go there. One more look will be enough…)
Lady Miko, can you hear me?
Yes. You may call me Hiyuki.
Understood, Lady Hiyuki.
...Mmm.
Most of the Left Behind in Honami City have been relocated. But a few, the ones whose regrets run deepest, still roam the danger zones.
Their unfulfilled desires concentrate the Lament's power. An incident just waiting to happen.
With you here, I feel better about the situation. We didn't expect you to take this on yourself...
Protecting Honami is the duty of the Miko of Flaming Sakura. I thank you for your support.
Actually, I have a favor to ask.
Oh, is it about Gyokuro, the former miko? We've already found traces that may belong to her, but nothing we can confirm yet.
We're in the process of narrowing down her location. As soon as we learn anything, you'll be the first to know.
I am in your debt.
Oh, it's nothing. If it weren't for you back then, I would have... Anyway, I'll leave this area to you!
You can rely on me.
…Was there something else you wanted to say?
...N-Nothing! Lady Hiyuki, it's just... Ugh, thank you for everything you do! Goodbye!

Hiyuki arrives in Honami and makes contact with a Black Shores agent to be briefed on the current situation before handling the deep-rooted obsessions of the Left Behind. The agent mentions that the exact whereabouts of Gyokuro, the former miko, remain unknown.
(This blade... It's as sharp as they promised.)
(Once business in Lahai-Roi is settled, I'll take it back to Ashinohara.)
You... You are...!
Yes.
Miko of Flaming Sakura, you're back! I knew those monsters wouldn't stand a chance!
Everyone else thought we were done for, but I told them, "Don't worry. Lady Miko will come and save us!"
...I'm sorry. I was too late.
It doesn't matter! You're here now, aren't you?
Please, just say it. Say the Miko of Flaming Sakura has returned, and that those monsters are finished…!
The Threnodian has retreated, but I couldn't... I couldn't save you...
But you defeated it. Again. And one day, you'll drive it away from Ashinohara for good!
Yes, I hear your wish. And I promise, one day...
Purge the Left Behind.
A young, excited Left Behind reminisces about his admiration and dependence on the Miko of Flaming Sakura's salvation.
(I... failed their hopes.)
(No matter how many times we drive it back, the Threnodian always returns.)
(When will we be freed from this nightmare, Ashinohara…?)
This is all your fault! "Eternal Miko," huh? …You're all useless! Every last one of you!
How do you even dare show your face in Honami…!
They're dead! Everyone's dead! Hahaha… I knew it. I knew the Threnodian couldn't be defeated… I should've run away as far as I could…
Lady Miko... Lady Miko! Please! Do something! Anything?
...I knew you couldn't hold the line. I knew it couldn't be killed, but... how do we go on from here?
Powerful people like you won't die even if you lose. What about us? What about ordinary people? Just wait to be torn apart?
Lady Miko... Heroes...! Tell me, what should we do?!
If you need someone to hate, let it be me.
It's alright. Place all your pain, all your resentment upon me. May your soul be granted a moment of peace.
Purge the Left Behind.
A sorrowful Left Behind spits bitter accusations at the Miko of Flaming Sakura's failure to protect the city.
(If I had been stronger… could I have saved them?)
(If only I had more power...)
Lady Miko... Are you the Lady Miko?
Oh! You are! You're the miko who once talked to Lady Gyokuro!
She told me about you. She said you're her most treasured family, just like my big sister and I! Your name is... um, it's...
Hiyuki.
Yes! Hiyuki, that's it! Such a pretty name, just like Lady Gyokuro's!
My sister said if I hid here and prayed with all my heart, Lady Miko would come and save us. So… I've been waiting.
Little one, what's your name? Your hands are cold. Have you been waiting long?
Kanna. My sister calls me Kanna.
Lady Miko... you're finally here, but my sister... She...
It's alright. I'm listening.
She asked you to wait here, didn't she? She must have trusted you very much to give you such an important task.
A... task?
Yes, a task. If not for you, I would never have found this place.
You did well, Kanna. You completed your task perfectly.
Lady Miko, are you here to save Honami? Then… is Honami safe now?
…Mm, Honami will be fine.
Sister said you carry everyone's wishes. That you're the strongest! I knew you'd come and fix everything!
Um… Lady Miko, can you help me find my sister?
Of course. We'll both get to see our sisters again soon.
Purge the Left Behind.
When we find her, let's eat red bean pudding! We make the best red bean pudding in the whole world! If you come try it, everyone will want some!
Then you'll need to make a lot of it. Can you manage that?
Pinky promise! We'll make lots and lots of pudding!
Then even if the Threnodian comes back, we'll have something to eat, and we won't be scared anymore!
It's a promise then. We'll make a lot of pudding.

While purifying the Left Behind, Hiyuki encounters a little girl named Kanna, a girl she once knew who crossed paths with both her and her older sister, Miko Gyokuro. Kanna has long since passed away, and like Hiyuki, she too lost a sister. 
To comfort her, Hiyuki promises to make delicious red bean pudding together. Kanna slowly dissipates before her eyes. Hiyuki watches in silence, the memories of the past lingering in the quiet.
(Red bean pudding... Gyokuro always did have a weakness for sweets like that.)
(This time... will I find her traces and truly set her free?)
Today's the anniversary of Threnodian's defeat, so we're getting off early. Dinner?
I need to pick up some Miko of Flaming Sakura merch. Plus a commemorative booklet. You know, for the little one at home.
Oh, please. We both know it's for yourself.
Ugh... that's so bitter. How do you even drink this?
Excuse you! The tea you drink is way worse. These are imported coffee beans!
Oh ho, so she's been hiding the good tea, has she? She just ordered two tins of top-grade gyokuro.
Hey, hey, don't! I was saving that for her birthday...
So, how's that new manga? I'm kind of burnt out on all the "fighting Threnodian" stuff.
I still like it. I mean, it's not like Threnodian's a solved problem in real life, right?
I know, I know. We get to take it easy 'cause the mikos are the ones carrying the weight for us.
Hm... maybe there shouldn't be too many stories like that. People might start thinking it's easy.
I saw the Lady Miko with my own eyes...
Just yesterday, I saw the Lady Miko of Flaming Sakura with my own eyes!
Flawless white hair. Sharp red eyes. And that sword—except it was only an empty scabbard!
They say she once broke off a Sakura branch from the roadside, turned it into a divine blade, and beat back the Threnodian with it!
The legend's gotta be true! She really is Ashinohara's strongest!
That sounds like the new manga...
Hmph! Then think about this, who published that manga? And why does the author only go by "Snow" and never show their real name?
There's only one explanation!
The author is obviously someone from inside the Flaming Sakura! That manga's basically a history book!
Come on, move! We'll miss it if we don't hurry!
Ugh... do we really have to listen to him talk? Can't we just go buy the manga?
According to the mission details, there's still one left.
Found the second one.
Copy. Efficient as always.
Everyone's on schedule. Don't rush. Stay alert.
Got it.
This is the last one, right?
Yeah.
All done. I'll send it to Lucilla and head back.
Third one secured.
Copy. Return to base. We're nearly done here.
Some sectors still aren't clear. How's everyone holding up?
All secure. We have teams handling cleanup. Just come back and regroup.
We're blazing through these runes. Teamwork makes the dream work, huh?
Right. I hope Sigrika is having the same luck.
So while you were searching, I tried tuning into each rune's frequency. Each trace felt different.
They're definitely real, but whatever's left behind is way too faint. Can't really feel what it is...
What's going on?
Ah, Mentor. You're back.
We've finished analyzing the runes, but their meaning still escapes us.
As for the overlapping coordinates, we've run multiple standard decryption algorithms, but the results are contradictory.
I'm no rune expert… but we have a one here, don't we? She resonates with them.
Who—wait... Me?!
You wanted to say something, didn't you?
Bingo.
…If you're asking me, these runes don't feel flat. They feel… spherical.
About this big. And these two points are relative… like this.
Three-dimensional coordinates... orthographic projection...
If we take the Reactor Drive's light data from the database as a depth layer and reconstruct it...
A map forms. The Royan runes are conveying a spherical map.
What are these two places...?
The one on the surface corresponds to the Stridergate. As for the one at the center... there's too little data to confirm.
But by geometric definition, it's the origin point of the entire map.
The Exostrider. That must be where it is in Voidspace.
It's the only thing that could survive there. The lodestar Aemeath was trying to show me.
Contamination around the Keystone is off the charts. We've got no viable spatial data. In other words, using the Stridergate to get you to Voidspace isn't an option.
I remember Lucilla mentioning a sealed rift in the Dimmr Plains.
Yeah. right. Once Hiyuki from the Special Response Force breaks the seal, that could serve as a new entry point.
But without the Exostrider... are you suggesting crossing the Stridergate and searching for it yourself, {PlayerName}?
Now that we have a concrete map, I should be able to retrieve the Exostrider before my power of Separation gives out.
...A compelling proposal. Hard to refuse.
But don't forget the Exostrider is currently out of power. Finding it is one thing. Extracting it is another.
Then we set a hard deadline.
If I can transmit the Exostrider's coordinates to you before Duskveil initiates—
Then tell me you'll consider entrusting the Drive Replica to us.
"Consider," huh... so you're handing the choice to us?
It's not about who's making the choice.
It's about trust.
I'm not asking for a guarantee.
I'm trusting you, the professionals on this side, to make the right call. Whatever that call is.
...The Exostrider's value justifies the risk. I agree to your proposal.

After successfully collecting all the Royan runes, Sigrika joins the decryption. She believes these runes were not drawn on a two-dimensional plane, but rather resemble spherical coordinates, possibly the coordinates of the Exostrider inside Voidspace. For this reason, you decide to venture back beyond the Gate. If you successfully recover the Exostrider, the Spacetrek Collective might entrust the Drive Replica to you and the Exostrider—you leave the call in their hands.
The Command Post only just pulled back from the Void Storm frontlines. Took longer than expected.
I've been briefed on the situation. Sorry I had to be absent for that.
No worries. It's all settled.
No worries. It's all settled.
Still, I'd like a word with you before you go.
Let's meet by the Research Institute.
Here. This is the Dimmr Plains map, updated with the latest intel.
Be mindful of the contamination. It's not just the Exoswarm or local lifeforms being affected. It's sealing off the terrain itself.
That said, where there's a will, there's a workaround. Hiyuki found one, though it took her some time.
That miko?
Yes. She's been investigating the Dimmr Plains in secret. You'll meet her there.
She also asked me to tell you that she's run into the Fractsidus there, including the missing girl, Denia.
Why would she want me to know that?
Mm... let's just say honesty is in her nature.
She just thought you might want to know. Perhaps her way of showing she has no hidden agenda?
Noted. I'll be leaving then.
Wait.
Look me in the eyes.
...Lucilla?
Relax. It's my Forte. You've seen it before.
I will temper this moment into a bullet of memory, carved with your name, your face, and all you have been.
What carries you is not only your own strength.
Thank—
Uh. Save that for when you come back.
Lucilla hands you the map of the Dimmr Plains. She tells you that Hiyuki is already there waiting for you.
(An entry point hidden in the Dimmr Plains... Time to get to Bjartr Woods.)
Chulu...
(It's already been totally contaminated by Voidmatter... Might be worse than I thought the lower I go.)
(But the frost seems to be slowing the spread. That frequency... it feels like Hiyuki's.)
(The Voidmatter contamination is bad... it's not looking good for the Soliskin.)
(This white bird... did Hiyuki leave it here?)
Alright. Lead me to Hiyuki.
(These ruins... looks like the Collective built them. Did the debris drift here after the destruction?)
(Hiyuki's tracks...)
(Something's off with these plants... Voidmatter influence?)
(...Where is it leading me?)
...Someone there?
She hasn't gone far. Probably watching us from the shadows.
She seemed... like her grip is slipping.
She was already like this when I last encountered her. Shortly before the Voidmatter surge.
Hiyuki, when you fought Denia, did you see anything like a shadowy hand?
Yes.
(Then Denia was behind the incident beyond the Stridergate.)
(Could she also be responsible for the Academy's Voidmatter surge...?)
Lucilla contacted me. She said you intend to return beyond Stridergate.
The roads here have been swallowed by Voidmatter. There is no direct passage.
Lucilla must have told you of the rift that appeared over a decade ago. Thinking back now... that was likely the Fractsidus's doing.
(Luuk said the Grand Architect caused that rift. That... was what led to Aemeath's death in the simulator cockpit.)
If they used a Threnodian frequency extracted from here to influence Denia... then the Spacetrek Collective was already infiltrated from the beginning...
The Threnodian's surge now could be their attempt to force the girl to master that power.
Or... perhaps that power within her has simply broken its leash.
Long ago, I sealed a portion of my own power there to help aid the Solvein Heartwood resist the Threnodian.
Break the seal, and you will pass through the rift to reach the Voidmatter depths beyond it.
Understood. I'm relying on you.
Let's go. Time isn't on our side.
Savior... who has always walked with victory. This time, have you come to save Lahai-Roi?

After repelling Denia's attack, you finally reach Hiyuki. Denia's mind appears fractured, and you suspect that she is connected to what you experienced beyond the Stridergate.
Hiyuki reveals that she used her power to seal the "rift" that led to Aemeath's death over a decade ago. Now, she tells you she will guide you to your destination.
That child's state... she did not appear to be under complete control.
It was Denia herself who stopped us.
Let's go. We need to get up there first.
...Got it.
Do you know Denia?
Lucilla asked me to watch over the students. I observed her. She did act strangely at first...
But in time... I could see her effort to belong. She did seem happy at the Academy.
Have you been watching over Denia here?
Yes... After I sensed the Threnodian's breach, I sent my Shiratori to trace it. That led me here, to her, in the Dimmr Plains.
She remains under the Fractsidus's sway, but her actions just now betray her own will.
She always hid in the Void Storms. I have no wish to harm her… So I did not find the right moment to intervene.
Wait here. This seal is wrought from the "Futures' Tithe." To put it simply, I drew upon countless versions of my future self and anchored them here to suppress the Voidmatter.
To break the seal is to reclaim those futures. That task is mine alone.
I understand. I'll leave it to you.
There is one thing... with Aleph-1 active, I may not be able to restore this seal once it is broken.
You choose the most perilous path at the most perilous time.
I know your strength. I know your history of victory.
To take such a risk… is it because you remain confident you will not fail? That you cannot lose to the Threnodians?
No. I've failed before. I don't think anyone can win forever.
If I'd never lost, maybe... I'd still have my memories. I wouldn't need to start over and Solaris would be free from the Lament.
I do this because I can. And because someone has to.
And what if you fail...?
Then you evacuate immediately.
Then Duskveil will proceed as planned.
I meant what becomes of you?
Maybe I'll be lost to Voidspace. Maybe I'll find a way to turn the tide. I can't know right now.
But if I don't try, there's no way I'll ever know.
What makes me scared isn't coming up short. It's the thought of not giving it my all.
The cost of this attempt is steep, and you bear the weight of many. I expected more caution.
To fail even once against a Threnodian is to invite apocalypse.
Even knowing this... you would still go?
Hiyuki. Lucilla told me you fought a Threnodian in Ashinohara for a long time.
I don't know how you did it, but I can only imagine what it took to hold the line in a place already so destroyed. So I'll ask you...
Did you fight in Ashinohara because you were certain you would win?
...No. I was not.
I never was.
Then you understand why I'm doing this. You would make the same choice in my place.
I see.
I will attend to the <te href=851078>Seal of Flaming Sakura</te>. It will not take long.

Before breaking the Seal of Flaming Sakura, Hiyuki mentions the high likelihood that confronting the Threnodian could end in failure. You don't deny it. You don't promise success, only that you'll give everything you have, just as she did back in Ashinohara. Hiyuki sighs, then turns toward the seal.
No need. I must do this myself...
Oh… Why not choose the easier future?
The Dark Tide...
Hiyuki!
Let me help!
The girl...
It's been a while, Miko of Flaming Sakura. And you as well, {PlayerName}.
I burned my future as a Threnodian Resonator. Your plan holds no meaning now.
Every step in our dance has its purpose, Lady Miko.
Our paths have crossed often, but in my design, this is our true introduction to each other.
Because at last, I have a "masterpiece" worthy of your attention.
You infiltrated the Collective for Denia and Hiyuki. You wanted  Leviathan's power... to merge them into one.
Denia was always a Fractsidus creation. She is honored to be used as intended.
A creation...?
The Mya disappointment made me think. If I could just create a superior vessel, one refined by a Miko's spirit, perhaps it might bear the full might of a Threnodian's power.
As for the outcome... you would know better than I, Lady Miko.
...Wordplay won't change your failure. Leave, you tedious avatar.
Failure? No. Listen you two, I'm just showing Solaris another "possibility."
If we made one Denia, we can make another. And another. Until we hold a complete, obedient Threnodian Resonator in our hands.
Your own borrowed future showed you that. A true Threnodian Resonator will exist.
Help... help me...
Tsk, tsk. All that desperate running on the wheel, and you still came up short. I'll have to throw you away.
She can deny it all she likes, but the void in her heart is the same as Aleph-1's. That emptiness... it will always pull her toward the Threnodian's power.
Chase her if you must. You do so love playing the "savior," {PlayerName}.
As for this avatar... I'll spare you the trouble.
Wasting time on one of their avatars is pointless...
Come on, Hiyuki. The seal is open. We have a job to do... Denia needs us.
If you wish, I can tell you about my history with the Fractsidus. But of course, Denia is the more pressing matter.
It's not hard to guess... The Grand Architect wanted your power as the Miko of Flaming Sakura, didn't they?
But like you said, you burned that one future as a Threnodian Resonator. You can be done with them.
Right now, Denia's state is what worries me.
I fear... that child may already be...

The Grand Architect appears before you again, this time with Denia in two, her eyes empty. The Grand Architect shows her off as one of her "masterpieces," an artificially created Threnodian Resonator. But Denia alone could not withstand the full might of Aleph-1's power, and is discarded by the Grand Architect as a "worthless creation."
You and Hiyuki continue forward toward the collapsed Denia.
Aleph-1... I curse you...
Can't... go inside...
Rest now.
Do not worry. The Voidmatter frequency within her has weakened. She will recover.
Thank you for looking after her.
Good. That handles things here.
Forgive me, but I believe waiting for the Voidmatter to spread would take too long. This will be faster.
Allow me to handle this. There is not much time left in your deadline with Mr. Chase.
My Shiratori can guide you through Voidmatter, but the concentrations here go beyond its strength.
So I will draw upon a future where I learned to fully channel its power.
It will protect you and help you find the path you seek.
The rest... is yours to face.
Thank you, Hiyuki.
{PlayerName}, I've read the Spacetrek Collective's report on Aemeath.
She wrote that her intention is... "Saving the world."
Lucilla said you are her family. I suppose you must be the reason she held that vision.
Do you regret it? Leading her to the "savior's" path? To a life of pain and struggle?
One must walk this path alone in order to protect.
And those who walk it out of admiration will lose themselves in the mire...
No, Hiyuki. I don't seek Aemeath to confess my regrets.
I want to find her, so I can tell her...
I hope to make you proud. I hope I haven't let you down...
I've always been proud of her.
She chose to walk this path with me, so I know I'm not alone. Should I ever fail, I know there will be someone who'll continue to protect Solaris.
With your extraordinary strength, that is indeed possible.
But for those who are too weak to save others, there is only futility waiting at the end.
...As I learned when I failed to save Ashinohara.
All I can do now is hold my Suzu tightly, to ensure that after Hiyuki... there will be no more Miko of Flaming Sakura.
That... is all that remains.
From Lahai-Roi to the distant lands of Jinzhou and Rinascita, people rise up to fight the Threnodians.
I've been part of that fight. But to pretend I'm the only reason they're standing? That everything fails if I do? That's arrogance. It's an insult to everyone else choosing to stand their ground.
My victories were never mine alone.
If even one person makes it to the end of this road, then we're still winning.
Hiyuki. When you're walking alone and feel that weight... Just stop for a moment. Look around.
Stop...
You'll see that there are people who draw strength from you. People out there who are proud of you.
And when you finally reach the end of your journey, you'll know it was never futile.
...How enviably brilliant.
Huh?
Come again?
It is nothing. I will tell you when you return.
All right. I'll be back soon.
I will hold the line here until Duskveil initiates. I will wait for you... together with everyone.

After defeating Denia, you prepare to journey beyond the Stridergate. Hiyuki hesitates before you go, wondering aloud whether a savior should walk a solitary path. Leading others down this road, she believes, only invites misfortune—just as it did for Aemeath. But you tell her that you have no regrets, and have always been proud of the person Aemeath is. Because of her, you no longer feel alone on this path.
Your words remind Hiyuki of her own sister, Gyokuro. Something shifts in her expression, and she confides that she is a little "jealous" of you, then tells you she will wait here for your safe return.
Exostrider...
Someone consumed by Voidmatter...?
Come here. The Exostrider's power is nearly spent, but I'll do my best to protect you.
This frequency...
No. Your existence is already nearly—
"Where... am I?"
"Did the Void Storm consume me? Am I... dead?"
"Is this Voidspace? A higher dimension?"
"Our existence... is fading..."
"Did you just say that time and space... have no meaning?"
"Who's speaking?"
"I can't see you... but I can feel you."
"Sound doesn't travel here. But thoughts do."
"The Exostrider?"
"What are you doing?"
"This is meaningless."
"That's enough!"
"It's futile..."
"Our lives already ended in the void."
"I heard the Exostrider's <ano=frequency>cry for help</ano>. I want to find it."
"Time and space don't mean a thing."
"You'll never reach it."
"I know what the Exostrider means to Lahai-Roi, but..."
"You want to save it? How?"
"Nothing makes sense here!"
"But I... we..."
"We made it to the stars to reach for the unknown, didn't we?"
You...
"This is where we came from."
A spacetime coordinate with the Exostrider as the origin? But that's impossible inside a black hole...
"Now, we will mark the past with the future."
...Thank you.
You charted the unknown with your lives. I'll make sure it's not in vain.
The light of the Origin Point... <ano=The Exostrider and I>We</ano> will bear it.
Aemeath?
I'm here.
Aemeath, can you bring the Drive Replica here? It's the "heart" they built for the Exostrider.
Don't worry. This time, we'll save the world together.
...Okay. I'll bring you back humanity's hope.
So... wait for me.
"Why is the Exostrider here?"
"What happened?"
"The light?"
"...What should we do?"
"Just another step on a long road of discovery."
Stars... Reached... Meaningless... Reason.
Over here, Aleph-1. Right now, you're dealing with me.
All beings... Dissolve. Waves... rise and fall. Destination... void...
Seek... Boundary... Destruction... Sighs.
The Exostrider's frequency is decaying...!
Status on the Drive Replica?
Almost ready. We just need the calibration data now!
Its reading is about to flatline!
Alert: Arbiter. Mech ascension complete. Systems at peak. Ready to reengage enemy.
Exostrider... you're awake?
Assessment: A "human heart" is now operational within me. I can now speak with their voice.
In frostlands where civilization should not have taken root, a collective will was born. A will to explore what lies beyond.
They... have chosen me.
That's our chance!
Verdict: Judgment is at hand.
Annihilation... Certain. Voyage... Futile. Beginning... Will?
Directive: I voyaged here to fulfill my mission. Life on this planet has the right to explore the vastness of the cosmos.
You see the civilizations across the stars only as food to quiet your hunger. You cannot comprehend the will that drives them.
The end... Void. Meaning... Suffering...
Opinion: Even if humanity's final frontier is an endless void, it is not proof of a meaningless pursuit, Aleph-1.
Confirmation: Battle concluded. Voidmatter responses within this sector have dissipated.
But... Threnodian Aleph-1 wasn't destroyed.
Analysis: Resonator Aemeath. Under current parameters, the permanent termination of Aleph‑1 is impossible.
Therefore, I exercised autonomous judgment and exiled it to the furthest reaches of this universe.
Request: Arbiter, permission to remain. Before your final judgement, allow me to submit a log of my experience in Lahai-Roi.
Go ahead.
Log Retrieval: On multiple return transits, I was detected by Aleph‑1. It pursued me, its objective to enter Solaris through the Stridergate.
It attacked. I fell... but Resonator Aemeath answered me with her power, and I was able to banish a part of Aleph-1.
I should have initiated a full system seal to await repairs. But I saw countless pairs of terrified eyes.
Discovery: The Roya in the frostlands stood helpless before the Threnodian's Voidmatter calamity.
I saw panic and dread. Following the protocol shared with the Sentinels, I extracted my Reactor Drive and encased it deep within the ice. Then I powered down to slumber.
Exception: A momentary reactivation occurred. Resonator Aemeath resonated with me a second time.
She required my power to mend the Stridergate rift. I complied.
That was when I became a digital ghost. But was that the "second time" we resonated... or the "first"?
Initial Assessment: Upon reactivation, standard procedure was to request your retreat. Direct engagement with Aleph-1 was a tactical irrationality.
But the "human heart" granted me a voice. The will of this civilization gave me the strength to stand with you.
Component Analysis: This Drive Replica forged by humans is crude and fragile compared to my original Drive.
I can feel each one of its pulse cycles. Violent, eroding itself with every beat.
The shrill, grating friction of wear...This is the first time I have ever heard "myself." A heartbeat bestowed upon me by "them."
With this heart installed, I have acquired capabilities previously undocumented outside the Sentinel mech series.
Projection: Given Threnodian Aleph-1's abilities, we must assume it retains the capacity to return.
Once the Stridergate is fully sealed, I will remain on this side as its guardian. I will bar its re-entry into Solaris.
Request: Arbiter. I was born from humanity's hope. Please permit me to remain here and protect them until this heart ceases to beat.
...Very well. Permission granted.
Query: Resonator Aemeath. Are you willing to accept a portion of my power?
When I first arrived in Lahai-Roi, your assistance allowed me to seal the Threnodian beyond the Stridergate.
Your current state is a consequence for which I bear responsibility. While I slumbered, you safeguarded Lahai-Roi in my stead.
Now, the death that once drew near has been severed. Henceforth, your companion will be an enduring "life."
Summary: I am experiencing the state defined as "guilt."
Hah! Oh Exostrider, you're really wearing your "heart" on your sleeve, aren't you?
But hey, I chose this path myself. Made my peace where it ends a long time ago.
As for your question... This savior's got a career to uphold. Gotta level up if I'm gonna keep kicking bad guy butt, right?
So your power? Hell yeah, I'll take it.
Authorization: Resonator Aemeath's agreement confirmed. Synchronization limit removed. Commencing Resonance.
Suggestion: Don't you dare feel "guilty." Thank you for living up to my hopes. So... let's make our goodbye a happy one, okay?
Detection: Arbiter. You have brought your Cassette.
Proposal: Please, allow me to take it. I wish to burn a frequency onto the tape, for you to return to Lahai-Roi.
What frequency is that?
Briefing: While traversing the stars at high velocity, my trajectory once intersected with a slow-moving probe.
Its hull was marked with Solaris script. Its launch date is estimated to have occurred a long time ago.
I intercepted and recorded the signal it has been continuously transmitting to Solaris. Its name is "The Farwatcher."
Objective: I wish to offer this recording as a gift to the people of Lahai-Roi.
Status: Stridergate closure imminent. Preparing to return the Arbiter and Resonator to Lahai-Roi.
Query: One last thing, Arbiter. Will you permit me to alter my designation to bear the name "Lahai-Roi"?
Of course.
Why?
Designation change complete. Thank you, Arbiter.
Objective: I wish to always be reminded of my heart's origin.
As you wish.
Designation change complete. Thank you, Arbiter.
Valediction: Farewell, Arbiter. Please deliver my temporary goodbye to the people who forged this heart.
Prediction—We will meet again in the boundless sea of stars.
Until we will meet again, in the boundless sea of stars.

Once the Drive Replica is embedded within the Exostrider, it completes its ascension and becomes a Sentinel. You and Aemeath pilot it together to defeat the mech forged by Aleph-1. But the victory is not permanent. The Exostrider warns that banishing Aleph-1 to the depths of the universe is only a temporary measure. It still retains the capacity to return one day. Because of this, the Exostrider chooses to bestow a portion of its power to Aemeath and elects to remain on the other side of the Stridergate, guarding the civilization that gave it a heart until the very end. Before you part, the Exostrider requests your permission to change its designation to bear a new name: "Lahai-Roi." It then leaves a gift for the people of Lahai-Roi inside your Cassette.
It's been so long since I last saw Solaris. Feels weirdly nostalgic.
Now that Aleph-1's been kicked to the cosmic curb, Lahai-Roi can finally breathe easy.
I was gonna say we saved the world this time. But honestly...

It was the explorers who fell into the Voidmatter. They're the ones who mapped that data on me and the Exostrider inside the Threnodian's black hole. All I did was burn their findings onto a Cassette for you.
And it was the people of Lahai-Roi who decoded the Cassette and forged the Drive Replica. That's what let the Exostrider finally cast the Threnodian out.
So... this wasn't a story about heroes saving the world. It was the world saving its heroes. Right?
Yes. And now… we're home.
You're back?
Yeah, I'm back now.
During Aleph-1's recent activity, impact was successfully contained to the Dimmr Plains.
Within the Plains themselves, Voidmatter concentrations have dropped significantly across most sectors.
Monitoring data from the Spacetrek Collective confirms that Threnodian markers within the region of Lahai-Roi have now fallen to near-zero levels.
Exostrider activity has fully ceased. However, related research into its technology will continue.
Mech units deployed by Startorch Academy have successfully neutralized all anomalous constructs activated by the Threnodian across Lahai-Roi.
The experience gained through this mission will carry forward.
In Bjartr Woods, the area closest to the Threnodian's attempted breach, partial structural damage has been confirmed. Overall, the situation remains well within control.
As Voidmatter levels continue to drop, the Roya will regain access to a broader and more stable range of territory.
Once shrouded in Voidmatter, Lahai-Roi now stands restored. This was made possible by the choice of every last explorer.
The road ahead is vast. We will reach the stars. And then, we'll keep going.
You made it!
Turns out, decoding what the Exostrider burned into your Cassette was way tougher than the Institute thought. They only just finished today!
Over here, {PlayerName}! Everyone's waiting for you!
What could it possibly be... Oh, I can't wait to find out.
The Farwatcher.
I have to admit, {PlayerName}, I was genuinely surprised when you handed me this tape and said that name.
You said it was one of the nine interstellar space probes launched before the Etheric Sea engulfed Solaris.
Correct. The nine stars on the Spacetrek Collective's emblem represent those probes.
Ever since then, Solaris never received another signal from them. Not a single one.
...Until today.
Professor Mornye, you may begin playback.
This... Sounds like white noise...
Judging by the Cassette's data, they're sounds the Farwatcher recorded while drifting through different galaxies.
Sounds that have been lost to us for centuries, silenced by the Etheric Sea.
So that's why... the Exostrider wanted to give these recordings to the explorers of Lahai-Roi today.
Because this is a gift from light-years away. Sent by the explorers of Lahai-Roi's past...
...Echoes from the sea of stars.

Some days after the crisis is resolved, you arrive at the Research Institute to join the others in analyzing the gift the Exostrider left inside your Cassette. 
It is white cosmic noise recorded by "the Farwatcher," one of nine interstellar space probes launched before the Etheric Sea engulfed Solaris. It is the voice of the stars, captured by explorers who came before, a message that, after an eternity, finally found its way home.
The Spacetrek Collective has taken in that Denia girl. Just as you wished, Grand Architect.
Good. Just let them care for the little doll for now. Such an extraordinary performance. I'm sure I can find another role for her later.
All retrieved data has been processed. Awaiting review at your leisure.
May I ask what you plan to do next?
Plans are progressing everywhere it seems. I've dallied in Lahai-Roi long enough. Time to stretch my legs.
Cristoforo, be a dear and run a message to Eris in the New Federation for me.
Understood. Though I suspect the Vice Architect will begrudge, once more, your sending me to disturb their precious quiet.
Oh... and will you require me to draft a prologue for your characters?
No need. I'll pay Phrolova a visit myself first. That will be all for now. This body tires so easily. Remember to give her a silent ending...
Grand Architect, with this phase of verification complete and full production proceeding… do we still have use for that miko?
That won't be necessary. Her future as a Threnodian Resonator has been taken. She no longer requires our attention.
Besides, she's failed one too many times. Drawn one too many futures from herself. And she still insists on walking the path of saving Ashinohara alone, with no intention of stopping.
Every step she takes only deepens that obsession.
After countless cycles of failure, she too will be reduced to ashes by her own vow. Just like her sister.
Ah... that reminds me. Zashiki‑warashi has brought us some rather interesting news.

In an empty classroom, the Grand Architect contacts the Overseers from various regions. She spares no thought for the Ashinohara miko she once sought to recruit. Hiyuki has already burned through every possible future as a Threnodian Resonator. One day, she too will be reduced to ash by her own wish, just like her sister Gyokuro before her.
(Gyokuro must be just ahead...)
(This was Honami's last Rite of Purging.)
(All is settled. Only one final thing remains.)
(I have truly... kept you waiting for too long, Gyokuro.)
Come then, Miko of Eternal Flaming Sakura.
Calm yourself.
A restless heart makes for unsteady hands.
But... I do not wish to learn this.
Then what do you wish to learn?
This girl will become a great archer.
No, look at her hands... they're a swordsman's hands.
Not in this life.
Her hands aren't ones to hold a blade. Isn't that right?
Well now. You have refined your technique.
Hmph.
Hiyuki...
Do not let the Suzu bind your fate.
Go seek a future of your own.
Focus, Hiyuki.
Survive this strike—
And reach your tomorrow.
Tsk, tsk. You have mastered many skills from others, but have yet to master the bladework of your own Flaming Sakura clan.
The last Miko withheld too much. She refused to teach me a single technique.
Do you resent her?
I do.
Even now?
Every moment.
I resent that she did not step down long ago. Instead, she burned every future she had and left nothing behind. Not even ashes.
I resent that she did not pass her skills to me. That she sent me away each time she trained. Now... there is nothing of her left in me.
Hiyuki...
I resent that she always told me to "be myself," yet chose a death that was nothing like herself.
And you resent that even after dying, she lingered as a spirit, waiting only for you to send her on.
Ah. I was being cruel, wasn't I?
It's just... I couldn't let my fear go. No one understood the scale of your gift, Hiyuki. Your potential to tame even a Threnodian.
If you drew such a future, would everything not become much easier?
Gyokuro, I...
As long as it comes from your own heart and will, any future you choose is acceptable.
But if our little Hiyuki truly became a Threnodian Resonator... Well. I'd have no choice but to follow "family law" and send us both on our way.
I have already burned that future.
I accepted the Suzu of my own will. I chose to become the Miko of Flaming Sakura.
I know why you refused to teach me your swordsmanship. You never wanted there to be another Miko after you.
I will fulfill that wish, Sister.
After Hiyuki, there will be no more Mikos of Flaming Sakura.
The Miko of Eternal Flaming Sakura... That is a heavy title to bear.
It is.
The future ahead is long. Very, very long.
I know.
Through the Suzu, we draw strength from countless possible futures.
But each time you do, you see yourself draw closer to Miko's destined end. A future burned to cinders, leaving nothing behind.
Yes. And before that destined end arrives, I will find a way to drive back the Threnodian.
So that Gyokuro may simply be Gyokuro. So that Hiyuki may simply be Hiyuki. So that Ashinohara will no longer need a Miko of Flaming Sakura.
You have grown up, our little Hiyuki.
I met someone, Sister. Unlike me, who failed Ashinohara, {Male=he;Female=she} always finds a way to save.
That must be difficult for you to bear, little Hiyuki.
Yes. At first... it was.
Hearing tales of {Male=his;Female=her} exploits, I felt a deep admiration. No, perhaps it was closer to jealousy.
I thought, if only I possessed {Male=his;Female=her} strength. Then my days would not be filled with regret. Ashinohara would be saved.
And I... in the peace that followed, could have lived an ordinary life.
But upon meeting {Male=him;Female=her}, I came to understand. Even a great "savior" carries many pains and failures.
And the path of a savior is walked not alone, but alongside countless others.
Gyokuro.
Yes?
I have always admired you as the Miko of Flaming Sakura. And I have always... held you in the highest honor.
Even if one day I am burned to ashes...
Because of you.... because of {Male=him;Female=her}... because they all exist... I am never truly alone.
I take great pride in you, too.
Go on, Shiratori. Fly to where your heart desires.
May the day come when you, too... step into spring as freely as anyone in this world.

Hiyuki tells Gyokuro how much she still misses her—the longing, the resentment, and finally her wish: to become the Miko of Flaming Sakura who defeats the Threnodian, so that Ashinohara will never need another miko after her.
But Hiyuki admits that meeting Rover changed something in her. She no longer despairs over countless failures. Though she knows a future awaits where she burns to ash, knowing she once had a companion on this path makes the pain easier to bear. 
Before Gyokuro fades away, she tells Hiyuki that she has always been proud of her.
Greetings, Miko of Flaming Sakura.
...You.
Oh, relax. I'm not here to recruit you. I just heard a piece of news and thought you might want to know.
Not long ago, that island floating over the sea, your hometown—
It vanished. All in a single day.
Congratulations on your wish coming true, Lady Miko.
A message arrives for Hiyuki from the Grand Architect: "Ashinohara has vanished in a single day. Congratulations on your wish coming true, Miko of Flaming Sakura."
The end... Silent...
Prove... Existence... You...
Hope... Stagnate...
Silence... Eternal...
Almost finished up here at the Academy. Heading back now!
I know you're busy, so no need to reply. Cya over at Professor Mornye's!
Friend sent a nudge
I was sent to search the area near the Academy.
...Be careful.
Contact me if you need any help.
Don't worry.
Copy. I'll leave that side to you.
Don't worry. I'll see you later.
See you once the mission is over.
Alright. See you then.
Mmm... the spring breeze is so warm.
So, how've you been feeling the last few days?
Much better, actually. I feel fine.
Still a little dizzy, honestly...
Hmph. You soaked in Voidmatter for that long... and you're telling me you're fine? Better not be lying to me.
...Being alone in Voidmatter that long will have taken a toll on your body, even with the white bird's protection.
Just thinking about what you risked... Hey, don't you ever do something like that again, okay?
I could say the same to you, Aemeath. You think what you did before was any less risky? "Never coming back" isn't exactly my idea of a happy ending.
That—ahem...
...Okay. I'm sorry, alright? Back then... I didn't have a choice.
You know how it is. Sometimes there are things you just have to do.
...I know.
But I still worry about you.
...I won't do it again. Promise. Cross my heart.
Besides, look at us now! We're both okay. And Lahai-Roi's safe! That's a pretty happy ending, right?
The savior and the world, saving each other at last! A tale for the ages!
Aemeath, there's something I want to ask you.
Hmm? What is it?
After you were sealed beyond the Stridergate... how long were you out there? Alone?
You've been dodging this. But I don't want you to keep choosing to handle this alone.
Aww... feels like if I try to brush this off again, I'm gonna get scolded for real.
...Honestly, I can't really remember anymore. Toward the end, I couldn't even tell if time was still moving.
It was just darkness. Emptiness. Over and over. Boring. Monotonous.
But hey, I've had practice being alone. It's fine.
The ones who faded into nothing... they were with me, you know. In all that dark, the light of every explorer who came before never stopped shining.
Knowing I was protecting the place they came from made it hurt a little less.
When I had the "Origin Point" and saw you, the gravity pulled me back and I... I couldn't go with you. That stung. But the moment I saw you? That made me so happy.
Seeing you again... that was enough.
Me too, Aemeath.
Okay, okay, this is getting way too heavy! Let's talk about something fun! You know, this whole thing is kind of a blessing in disguise.
People could only see me before. But now... I can "pilot" this shell to actually interact with people.
Pilot...?
Mm, kinda like being in my mech form? I mean, I did die. Having a shell doesn't mean I'm alive again.
But it's similar to the Exoswarm, and since I'm the Exostrider's Resonator... I'd say "piloting" this body feels about right.
And because of that, I've decided to stick around for a while and finish my studies!
For a while?
Yep! The Threnodian thing in Lahai-Roi is already handled, and I wanna be a real savior who helps more people. So after this... I'm gonna travel around Solaris.
But first, I'll stay at Startorch to graduate. I mean, I never even got my degree before I turned into a digital ghost!
Gotta give my school days a proper send-off, y'know?
Aemeath.
Yeah?
Even if it isn't a happy, carefree life... If it's the one you choose, I'll be proud of you.
So go follow your heart, Aemeath. I'll always be right here.
...Okay. I won't forget.
But before all that—walk with me? Just us and the spring breeze!

You find Aemeath and finally talk to her about what happened. She tries to deflect at first, but eventually yields under your steady gaze. The two of you speak of the helplessness of goodbye, and the terrible loneliness she endured during her long wait beyond the Stridergate—until the moment you found each other again in the darkness. What happened, she admits, turned out to be a blessing in disguise. She can now complete her studies at the Academy in a physical form. 
You tell her that even if life isn't always happy or carefree, as long as it's the one she chooses, you will always be proud of her. 
Aemeath gives you a relieved smile. 
She reaches out her hand, inviting you to walk with her. Spring, at last, no longer exists only in dreams.
Friend sent a nudge
The Collective wants to discuss the handling of the Aleph-1 aftermath with you.
If you're free, meet me at the Academic Affairs Office.
Understood.
Got it.
Understood. I'm on my way.
Got it. I'll head over now.
Wait... Something just came up. You know, the usual pointless reporting and corporate rhetoric.
Hiyuki will meet you there instead.
..Hiyuki?
Lucilla just left. There are still matters at the Academy regarding your identity that needed handling.
She had wanted to discuss the Collective's next steps with you. They've buried the Exostrider's disappearance with an official story, but as for who activated it... they want to keep it a secret.
That's the right call.
A cautious approach is smart.
I'll be leaving Lahai-Roi before long. The fewer eyes on me, the better.
Yes. She said that is the best way to protect you. Students will talk, that cannot be helped. But officially, the line is that the Exostrider "awoke on its own."
A wise choice. Fame is no blessing. If the Collective draws scrutiny from other regions, your freedom may not remain your own.
That was all she wished to convey, but I waited here to tell you something else.
Hiyuki recounts her encounters with the Fractsidus, and the situation in Ashinohara…
She speaks as evenly as always, but between her words lingers a sigh as quiet as falling snow.
Ashinohara…
…How are you holding up?
Do not worry over me. As for Ashinohara... Lucilla will assist with the investigation. Once matters here are settled, I will return.
But you must be careful. The Fractsidus seem to track your every move.
Their plans for Denia and me may have failed, but it proves one thing. They have found a way to harness Threnodian power.
Spying from the shadows... that does sound like them. Thank you, I'll be careful.
By the way, how is Denia?
She is still undergoing treatment and remains unstable. The Collective seem to have special arrangements for her. I wished to visit, but Lucilla said I am the one person who cannot...
Well, it was your blade that struck her down.
In that strike, I used the frequency you left within her. The one that "separates." That was what freed her from the Threnodian's control.
So in her eyes, you are likely just as frightening as I am.
Still, I hear her disposition may have changed. I should like to visit her one of these days... Hm.
Lucilla has more reports for me, something about Ashinohara. I should go.
Okay. If I can help with Ashinohara, just let me know.
Rest easy. As the "savior" {Male=himself;Female=herself}  taught me, this long road to save Ashinohara was never meant to be walked alone.
Go on.
...Perhaps I too can walk toward the spring you spoke of.

Hiyuki speaks to you about matters concerning Ashinohara, the Fractsidus, and her future path that she never had the chance to share before. She feels changed by everything that has happened.
Before you part, her white bird flies into your chest and disappears. Hiyuki gazes out the window, looking forward to that distant future of stepping into spring for the first time.
Found the first one.
Copy. You're in the most dangerous sector now. Be careful out there.
I know.
The light of the Origin Point... <ano=The Exostrider and I>We</ano> will bear it.
Finally came to see me?
Yes. I have come.
Made up your mind?
I have.
Ready to kill me, then?
...Yes.
Oh? Has my little Hiyuki grown so confident that she can best me without even drawing her blade?
Now, now... Don't forget who taught the Miko of Eternal Flaming Sakura. Can't show a little respect to your elder?
You call what you did to me teaching, Gyokuro?
Please note that the following requires no reply.
Mission parameters have been set. All units are to proceed to your assigned sectors now. Secure the Royan runes at your coordinates and report back via Wavesline.
As I mentioned before, we've divided everyone based on your expertise and field experience.
The Lahai-Roi subterranean zone is divided into the Academy, the Research Institute, and Bjartr Woods. Each unit will go to the sector matching their expertise.
I have full confidence in your abilities, and I ask that you place your trust in our strategic deployment. Return to the Research Institute the moment your missions are done.
To our Roya partners and Spacetrek Collective personnel: by choosing to leave the shelters and stand with us now, you have proven your readiness to sacrifice for the cause.
However, this rune recovery plan is not a march toward martyrdom.
Complete your missions within the time limit. Should any unforeseen situations arise, abort the mission immediately and return to the Research Institute.
Please remember that your teammates are right behind you.
And finally, just as you've all hoped, we are now forging a path toward the future of Solaris.
For that future, no price is too high.
Even if the Void takes everything, the will of humanity will never be snuffed out.
My highest regards to you all.
Updating current progress now. Royan rune collection is on schedule. We've received reports of injuries with medical teams already en route to those coordinates.
A one-way public channel is now open. We'll use it to keep you all posted on any developments.
Research Institute Sector collection is nearly complete. Academy Sector and Bjartr Woods Sector are at fifty percent.
All units that have completed your missions, report back to the Research Institute immediately.
Team 7 returned.
Team 10 is preparing to return.
Team 14 returned.
Team 22 returned.
Team 20 returning with Team 17 and 19. Team member Nastasha is injured.
Team 4 returned!
Team 2 confirms return.
Team 9 returning now!
Copy that. Please stay safe.
Collection complete for Research Institute, Academy, and Bjartr Woods Sectors.
All units that have completed your missions, report back to the Research Institute immediately.
Team 6 confirms return.
Team 3 confirms return.
Team 8 confirms return.
Team 18 and 13 confirm return.
Team 5 confirms return.
Communication with several teams is currently blocked. Specialized Security Teams have been deployed for search and rescue.
All units except for Team 11 have been intercepted by Security and are returning to base. Vital signs for all Team 11 members remain stable.
Well done, everyone.
Please report to Dr. Luuk and N.A.N.A. for medical evaluation, then return to the shelter at once.
Analysis of the Royan runes has already begun. You have all done your best. Now, it's our turn to uncover the secrets hidden within them.
Are you back?
Yeah...
I'm back.
Still trying to wrap my head around everything.
Yes, I'm back. About the Exostrider...
It's alright. We already know. Your safe return is what matters now.
Anyway, come meet us at the Research Institute. Everyone's been waiting for you.
Got it.
It's past the estimated waiting time... But I know you'll be alright, Mentor.
Voidmatter concentrations are already above the threshold. We have to retreat to the Institute's shelter now. Please head there as soon as you are back.
Mentor, I was just approved to stay and ensure that the Collective's emergency protocols proceed as planned. I'll be waiting for you at the Institute.
Mentor, you're back!
Yes, I'm back.
We were just in contact, but yes... I am back.
Must've been signal interference in Void Space. I only just received your messages.
I see... I'm just glad you're back. We can talk about everything else once you get to the Institute. Are you feeling alright?
I'm fine.
Don't worry, I feel perfectly fine.
That's a relief... In that case, come over to the Institute first.
Still not back yet?
Voidmatter concentrations just spiked. Profs are locking everyone down at the Institute.
I know you'll be fine. We've got rides waiting. You wouldn't bail on that, would you?
Voidmatter concentrations have passed the safety limit. The Spacetrek Collective is already starting the evacuation of Lahai-Roi.
I am staying behind for you. To the very end.
I'm back.
Are you still in the underground sector?
Glad to know you are alright.
How's the situation on your end?
How's the situation on your end? Need any help?
It is proving difficult. As the Voidmatter intensifies, locating people has grown increasingly challenging...
Do not worry about me. Go. Join Lucilla and the others.
{PlayerName}... Where are you now?
Friend sent a nudge
{PlayerName}, I've been asking everyone. Even Lynae was being vague about it, but I figured it out…
You're in Voidspace, aren't you? Just like when we first arrived at the Academy.
You made it out safely last time. I know you'll make it out this time, too.
There's a Void Storm in the Academy right now...
{PlayerName}, I'm at the Research Institute. Professor Mornye is here, too.
I am confident that you'll be alright... You always find a way to handle whatever comes your way.
Senior, Voidmatter concentrations are critical! We're all moving to the Spacetrek Collective's shelter now.
There's someone helping my tribe evacuate, and right now I'm filtering and archiving all historical records from the Solisylum for separate storage.
You must be very busy now, right? I'll see you when you get back!
You needn't worry about us. We're handling everything on this end.
I know you'll come through, just like you always do. I'll be waiting for the good news.
Lament. Lament. Lament.
Hello, {Male=golden boy;Female=golden girl}.
Now that the Dark Side incident is wrapped up, manage any rest after the ceremony?
Things will get busy again soon. You'll need to stay focused, but not wound up like a spring. Stress leads to bad calls.
I've rested up. Don't worry.
I can't really relax until this is over.
I've rested up. Don't worry.
Good. I'm a psychiatrist, after all. Happy to know my patient is actually following my advice for once.
I can't really relax until this is over.
I see. I sympathize, but can't say I approve. Once this is all settled, remember to give yourself a break.
As for the Exostrider plan we discussed earlier, there's been a development.
Come to my "workspace." It's better discussed where the walls don't have ears.
Candy? It's a new flavor. Sigrika and Lynae suggested it. Great for lifting spirits.
...Mint jelly.
Yep, that'll wake you up.
Alright, Luuk. What's the word on this Exostrider plan?
My conversation with Lucilla went well. She's on your side. Your showing during the Dark Side incident also helped speed up the Academy's internal review.
That said, you've been a student here for less than a year. You'll need the Spacetrek Collective's approval to officially activate the Exostrider.
Their biggest financial backer is the New Federation. And of course, they answer to an entire board of investors.
And to them? The Exostrider is definitely a "high-value asset."
I've been flooding their desks with reports. Your Academy investigation record, your latest test scores from Rabelle College, and a full physical adaptability eval I wrote myself...
And? Were they rejected?
We got a meeting. But when that much money's involved, conflicts of interest make everything move very slowly.
Unlike Jinzhou or Rinascita, they've got far too many "cooks" in the kitchen.
I'm worried we don't have that kind of time.
Exactly. Which is why we're going to strike first. Besides, things have changed.
It all comes down to this guy.
This is Chase. Head of the Spacetrek Collective. He just got back to Lahai-Roi on a Triumph Avionics ship from the New Federation.
President Lucilla, Professor Mornye, and I have all put in a good word. You'll be meeting him at the Research Institute.
Chase started as a low-level employee at Ashridge Energy in the New Fed. Then he dropped off the grid for a few years before resurfacing in a government position.
After that, he left the government and started diving into multiple large-scale investment projects. Some say he's still been Ashridge Energy's man the whole time, but no one's been able to prove it.
He's sharp, low-profile, and very smooth. At his age, he's seen it all. His only real concern is if the Exostrider's pilot can be controlled.
Normally, they'd pick Academy Synchronists who've been signing stacks of NDAs since day one. But—
Sounds like his attitude changed? Does that have something to do with the "changes" you mentioned?
Come in. Let's talk over some hot drinks.

You enter Luuk Herssen's workspace to discuss piloting the Exostrider. Luuk tells you that the Academy's procedures have already been handled by him and Lucilla, but you still need to win over the board of investors from the New Federation.
The Head of the Spacetrek Collective, Chase, has recently returned to Lahai-Roi from the New Federation.
I heard Lucilla asked for your help after the ceremony. How did it feel working with her?
Well, at first she was pretty dominant, steering the conversation the whole time…
But now I understand it's because this Academy means so much to her.
Based on your intel, the Reactor Core has been returned. Ideally, we'd wait for the Exostrider to awaken before trying to help Aemeath or confront the Threnodian.
But, recent readings from the Institute's Stridergate monitors... they're off the charts.
Voidmatter reactivity is spiking. The Spacetrek Collective is getting twitchy. If Threnodian Aleph-1 breaks the seal, all of Solaris will be at risk, not just Lahai-Roi.
Now, the Collective didn't establish itself here without binders full of contingencies for a Threnodian event.
Problem is, every one of those plans would scorch decades of scientific progress.
Which, in our case, actually gives us some leverage. Enough to have a real conversation with their head.
... Then I'll convince him. I'll show him the lowest casualty count is the only one that makes sense. He'll have to approve me as the Synchronist who pilots the Exostrider.
Luuk, you mentioned you have contacts in the Black Shores. And this workspace of yours... It's secure, right?
Guaranteed. What are you thinking?
Lahai-Roi is closed off from the outside world. Public comms are probably tapped by Fractsidus moles inside the Academy. And right now... I need a direct line to the Black Shores.
I see. Give me a moment. I'll establish the link and leave the rest to you.
I received your call, {PlayerName}.
...Is the situation in Lahai-Roi that dire? You look exhausted.
...A lot has happened, Shorekeeper.
I'm alright. Just a little tired.
My frequency loss has been resolved. But Lahai-Roi is under imminent Threnodian threat. I need your help, Shorekeeper.
Understood. For this purpose… both I, and the Black Shores, exist.
Any additional intel on the Exostrider within the Tethys System? Verified or not, I need everything you have.
I trust Luuk. You can speak freely.
Records confirm that Luuk Herssen has been providing critical assistance and intelligence to the Black Shores. I trust your judgment.

After the founding of Startorch Academy, most information fell under their seal of secrecy. However, in its early days, you mediated negotiations between the Academy and the Roya. Through that process, the Black Shores gained partial access to certain archived insights.

The Spacetrek Collective fears that due to the Exoswarm's division, the Exostrider, even upon awakening, might not have enough power to face the Threnodian. This concern led to the Augmented Resonance initiative—the Synchronists.
Observing how Sentinels work with their Resonators, they theorized that the Exostrider, sharing similarities, could amplify its power through a Resonator as well.
However, considering your link with the Exostrider, the Tethys System does not recommend this method.
We have intel that deep beneath Lahai-Roi lies the <te href=851076>Keystone</te> left behind by the Exostrider long ago.
It rests directly atop the seal on the <te href=851077>Stridergate</te>. You can think of it as a kind of lock.
You do not need a cockpit to pilot the Exostrider. So long as its activation sequence remains unobstructed, you may pilot it using the Keystone.
...Is it because I'm its "Arbiter"?
Precisely. For one who shares its origin, a cockpit would be more of a hindrance.
As for Aemeath... I think Dr. Luuk's proposal is viable.
You mean using the Exoswarm-like "shell" that appeared in the cockpit to look for her?
Yes. The connection between frequencies will help. {PlayerName},  bring "her" with you when you activate the Exostrider.
Sure. While I'm there, the Black Shores will need to keep a closer eye on Lahai-Roi.
I understand.
...I'm counting on you, Shorekeeper.
As long as I may be of help to you, I am… content.
Please return safely from the Stridergate, {PlayerName}.
I will. Don't worry.
I'll inform Lucilla. Take your time finishing that drink before you head out.
What do you think? I'm about to ask a man to sign off on a crazy plan, and I can't even promise him it'll work.
Nobody has a plan they promise will work. If Chase agrees, it's because he's betting that you're his best option. Just take the win.
At the end of the day, it all comes down to the person, {PlayerName}.

Luuk tells you about unusual Voidmatter activity near the Stridergate, an opportunity that can be used to persuade the Spacetrek Collective of the necessity of your plan. To make preparations, you contact Shorekeeper from Luuk's private workspace, who, with the help of the Tethys System, informs you that you can pilot the Exostrider by activating the underground "Keystone."
Shorekeeper also notes that Aemeath's "shell" likely connects to her frequency beyond Stridergate. She suggests that you bring "Aemeath" with you when you go.
I believe Dr. Luuk has already briefed you about the meeting with the Spacetrek Collective's head.
Come to Academic Affairs. He's waiting for you there.
Mr. Chase. This is the student I mentioned, {PlayerName}. Our best hope for piloting the Exostrider.
Save the intro, President Lucilla. We've met before.
(Before…? In my past life at the Academy?)
Let's just get down to it. The Threnodian Aleph-1 situation is deteriorating. We're running out of time.
I wanted to wait for the Exostrider's self-regeneration to finish, but the clock has run out on that one.
So tell me, {PlayerName}. Why do you want to pilot the Exostrider? What's the play?
To find Aemeath and put down the Threnodian.
Aemeath… Name rings a bell. The one with the high-sync rate. Her disappearance was a real loss for us.
She's beyond the Stridergate.
...Hehe. I see how it is.
But finding one person in a goddamn interdimensional haystack? Not so easy. You understand our position... the Threnodian is our priority.
Stopping the Threnodian is my priority too. I'm gonna handle both.
Look, we have the same objective, so like you said let's "just get down to it." If Voidmatter erupts, Lahai-Roi is ground zero. Every one of your projects will take a hit.
Regarding Aleph-1, our intel is insufficient. We are not, at this time, considering its total destruction.
What we need is for the Exostrider to reinforce the seal on the Stridergate.
To do that, we need a qualified Synchronist. And we need the Institute's Drive Replica. When that gets embedded in the Exostrider's chest cavity, then its repair is complete.
Right now, the Replica is in its final phase of development. We're still missing critical data.
A Drive Replica... Is that what you're building from the fallen Reactor Drive after Helios was launched?
Heh, correct. It's based on the original Drive's framework. You had a hand to play in that. You made sure Helios got launched.
To complete the Drive Replica, we require precise data from the Exostrider operating in high concentrations of Voidmatter, and environmental readings from inside the Threnodian.
Without them, we can't guarantee the Exostrider can resist Aleph-1's corruption.
Here's the verdict, {PlayerName}. On behalf of the Spacetrek Collective, I authorize you to pilot the Exostrider.
But remember, our objective is the data. We need readings from the high-concentration zone to complete the Replica.
And your personal mission gets ten minutes. That's the max duration our Voidmatter Barriers can cover the Stridergate once it's opened.
After ten minutes, you return to Lahai-Roi. Whether you found Aemeath or not. Do you accept?
Deal. One more thing. I won't be using a cockpit. Just show me the way to the Keystone and have the Exostrider ready. That's all I need.
Heh. Still full of surprises. Always bringing us the... unconventional.
Lucilla, run a high-pressure sync test for {PlayerName}. I've seen the physical eval, but I want live data for validation.
The moment the numbers hit green, we'll begin preparations for activating the Keystone and deploy Voidmatter Barriers around the Stridergate.
There's something else. I need a favor.
Go on, {PlayerName}.
The Fractsidus is lying low. Even after failing in the Dark Side, they could move the moment the Stridergate opens.
Ah. That's what's on your mind. Well, I have an update. Remember Sigrika's friend? The… somewhat lackadaisical one?
You mean Denia?
That's the one. She's gone missing. Our investigation points to her being the agent the Grand Architect referred to.
But don't let it distract you. I have a reliable friend keeping an eye on Fractsidus activity.
You just focus on waking the giant. Leave the rest to us, {PlayerName}.
The Black Shores just sent a formal communication request. They're offering full support for Lahai-Roi's evacuation, if it comes to that.
You handle it, Lucilla. You have a better read on the situation than I do. And you're better at talking with that {PlayerName}.
Heh... If there's anything else you want to ask me, now's the time.
I'm surprised you're placing this much trust in {PlayerName}, authorizing {Male=him;Female=her} so readily. Any particular reason?
We're different, President. I'm a businessman. And businessmen trust their instincts for a good investment.
The Threnodian could strike at any time. Given the circumstances, Lahai-Roi may have to be written off.
And with the timeline we have, relocating the Exostrider is impossible. If we're going to lose it anyway, why not let {PlayerName} take one last swing?
Understood. We still need to prepare for the worst-case scenario.
Of course. If this fails, we'll initiate the backup plan—Duskveil.

Lucilla introduces you to Chase. The shrewd old man states plainly that he is more concerned with the Drive Replica's development, and hopes it will give the Exostrider the capability to reinforce the seal on the Stridergate. If you pass the Synchronist Test, he agrees to let you pilot the Exostrider. But you are only given ten minutes to achieve your personal goal of finding Aemeath.
After you leave, Chase remarks to Lucilla that the Black Shores already submitted an offer for assistance. The decision to trust you was based on a businessman's eye for opportunity. But if you fail, the Spacetrek Collective will abandon the Exostrider and initiate "Duskveil."
Ugh, that guy gives me the creeps...
What kind of place is the New Federation, anyway? Are they all gonna be like that?
We'll find out when we get there, Abby.
So, you're {PlayerName}, huh? I'm Sakya. I'll be overseeing and evaluating your test today.
Lucilla filled me in on everything.
This synchronization test is far more intense than the standard procedure. We're simulating the neural load of a direct link with the Exostrider.
If you feel anything off, you call it. I won't allow the test to continue under Overclocking conditions. Review and confirm the safety protocols before entering the cockpit.
(Read and confirm the safety protocols.)
Good. The cabin systems are configured. The simulator cockpit is right there. I'll be watching your metrics closely the whole time.
I hope... you pass the test smoothly.
Neural network connected. Tactical model constructed.
Resonator vital signs... synchronized.
Neural load rate: fluctuations within normal range. Optimizing model... Ratio adjustment complete.
User status stable. No Overclocking detected.
Firepower threshold limit removed.
Energy output: Stable. Mech compatibility: High.
Test complete. Recording synchronization rate data.
Cockpit opening. Please exit according to the safety protocols.
...How did you get all the way here from the lake house?
