She wanders into the Academy sometimes. Usually, her caretaker bot escorts her back pretty quick, but today she was… being difficult.
We'll go back together in a bit, okay?
Hmph... So the little terror can be quiet. Never thought I'd see the day.
Professor Sakya, you knew Aemeath?
(I think I've heard this Professor's name before...)
Yes. I was her teacher. Over ten years ago now. She used to drive me up the wall. Made me lose my composure more than once. She never did seem to mind.
(Back at the fan meeting… people mentioned a Sakya. They said she was Aemeath's teacher. That she actually really cared.)

The last day I taught her, she wrote "Saving the world" as her career path. I asked her if that was really what a Synchronist should be aiming for, then gave her a piece of my mind and told her to rewrite it.
She didn't even get upset. Just smiled, took the form, and walked away. She never stepped out of that cockpit again.
Now she's back, but not really. What a headache of a student.
Lucilla told me you're her family. Is that true?
Yes.
And you're planning to pilot the Exostrider and bring her back from the Stridergate?
That's the plan.
Sounds like an absurd, bold, utterly delusional idea. No one knows what's actually behind that Gate.
I know.
...When Aemeath went missing, the cockpit recorded an abnormally high synchronization rate. We couldn't verify it after she disappeared, so it was never officially logged.
Your test results are just shy of hers overall. But in some sectors, they're even higher. You've passed, {PlayerName}.
I hope that absurd plan of yours works… and that you bring my student home.
No doubt about it.
...I'll do my best.
The results are out, but preparing the Exostrider will take time. You'll need to wait around two days.
We'll let you know when it's ready.
...Cas...sette...

After the Synchronist Test, you see "Aemeath" waiting by your simulator cockpit. Professor Sakya, who conducted your test, reflects on the Aemeath she once knew. She hopes that you will be successful in bringing back her student.
Later, alone in a chair, "Aemeath" suddenly whispers the word "Cassette."
Mentor, it's me.
The Exostrider has been primed for activation. The data reception framework for the Drive Replica is ready as well.
At your request, the Spacetrek Collective transferred "Aemeath" to the Institute… but she won't respond to anyone else. I believe she's waiting for you.
I'm at the Institute too. See you soon.
So, we're finally going to where that giant eye is hiding?
Yeah. And Abby, if you think it's stronger than you, don't try to be a hero, okay?
I've got this! But uh... We don't know how much strength it's hiding behind that Gate, huh...
Our priorities are the data and Aemeath. A direct fight with Aleph-1 can wait until the Drive Replica is ready.
Hey {PlayerName}! Are you... leaving now?
How did you know?
Were you... waiting for me?
I kinda cornered Professor Mornye. Bugged her until she cracked.
Yeah, I was worried. Figured I'd catch you before you left.
{PlayerName}, so you're gonna pilot the Exostrider.
You... feel nervous at all?
I know you're awesome and all, but nobody's ever done this before.
Nobody knows if the Exostrider will even move, or what else might go wrong.
Everything's felt so heavy at the Academy lately. Teachers are stretched thin. Even the nearby Exoswarm seem restless...
Lynae, are you nervous?
...Eh?! Okay, fine. Is it that obvious?
Look, after I aced the entrance exam, I thought, "Sweet. Life's gonna be packed again. Non-stop." Like it used to be.
But just when I was ready to finally be a student... my bubble popped. My brilliant student life, "Poof" and done.
The Threnodian, Voidmatter, the Fractsidus... They're breathing down our necks, and I can't do anything to help you fight them.
I just have to stand here and watch you walk into danger alone. It makes me feel totally useless.
I'd feel worse if I dragged everyone in Lahai-Roi into danger, just to back me up.
Of course, if things get really desperate, we'll have to face it together.
But for now? When I look at all the students who came here chasing their dreams... I want them to stay students.
Because...
It's a "savior's" job to protect the world.
Protecting the kids is a job for a grown-up.
Dang, that's a heavy title! ...We're in the same year, y'know.
Come on, you're a student too... We're just kids to you, or something?
…But I get what you're saying.
Making you waste time on a pep talk when you've got a world to save... I do need to grow up.
But I won't just sit around. I'll be on standby. Whatever the Academy needs to support your mission, I'm in.
And when this is all over, we're going for a drive!
Sounds nice.
Maybe we can start a biker squad next time.
Heh, can't wait for that day to come.
Professor Mornye's up ahead. Good luck out there, my friend!
You run into Lynae, who is worried and visibly anxious about the risk you are taking. You reassure her, promising another bike ride together once this is all over.
Temporary passage is complete. It'll take you directly to the Keystone.
The Black Shores will keep a team on standby here at the Institute. If you need our support, just say the word.
Thank you. We couldn't have built the data framework this quickly without your team's expertise.
Lahai-Roi is a top priority. A joint call by the Shorekeeper and the Tethys System... Oh. Professor Mornye, your guest has arrived.
Ah... you're here.
Is that the way to the Stridergate?
Yes. This temporary passage will take you to the Dimmr Plains, deep within Bjartr Woods.
Both the Stridergate and the Keystone are located in the Solvein Heartwood at its center. President Lucilla has already departed with the advance team. They'll be waiting on the other side.
And also...
Huff—huff—Sh-she's… too fast!
What happened?
Professor Mornye, she suddenly bolted into the Cassette Depository and... and took this.
This is...
Can I see it?
I'll give it back in a moment. Mornye, do you see what's special about it?
This ID number... I saw it in the student file during her inspection. This is Aemeath's own Cassette. It's been stored in the Depository since she went missing.
Why did you want to bring this now?
It may be instinctual.
Dr. Luuk did say this Exoswarm-like "shell" shares a frequency link with the real Aemeath.

Or it could be the "shell's" residual instinct. Cassettes are proof of a student's identity. Or it could be a weak signal sent from the Aemeath beyond the Gate. We can't determine which theory is correct yet.
However, this does remind me... carrying a Cassette into an unexplored Void Storm zone follows standard exploration protocol. Do you have yours?
Yeah. I've kept it with me.
I'll head to the Dimmr Plains now then, Mornye.
...I'll have to stay here, monitoring the data and ensuring the Drive Replica is ready. I wish I could go with you.
But I'll be fully supporting your mission. Mentor, please... take care.
I count on you to watch my back, Mornye.
With what we accomplished with Helios, I trust you'll handle the Drive Replica. We will succeed again.
I won't let you down, Mentor. Let this be... a new promise between us.
Let's go.

Mornye informs you that the passage to the Dimmr Plains has been completed, and that preparations to awaken the Exostrider have been completed. But during the inspection, "Aemeath" suddenly ran off alone to the Cassette Depository and retrieved the Cassette from before she became a digital ghost, holding it tight.
Mornye ensures to provide full support at the Institute while you collect data beyond the Gate—a new promise between you and her.
Ugh, stinky, this place reeks! The smell of that giant eyeball's getting stronger!
That massive glowing tree… is that the Solvein Heartwood?
(That miko...)
Wait. Before you go, you should say hello to {Male=him;Female=her}.
This is Hiyuki, I told you about her before. The only reason we could set up the monitoring equipment so quickly is because she cleared the way for us.
See that lingering frost? Ten years ago, a Voidmatter rift opened in the Dimmr Plains. Hiyuki went there alone and sealed it.
Thanks to her efforts, the Spacetrek Collective and the Roya were able to fortify the seal successfully.
Every task the Academy assigns her, she completes flawlessly. Were she to have a flaw though, it'd be that she goes through blades like nobody's business.
You were worried about the Fractsidus, weren't you? While you're beyond the Stridergate, she'll handle perimeter security.
She's hunted both the Threnodian and the Fractsidus for a long time. There's no one better for the job. Right, Hiyuki?
Yes. Those two Fractsidus members... I will track them down.
Here. Take this.
The white bird carries my power. It will shield you.
Should you lose yourself in the Voidmatter, it will also guide you back.
Hiyuki is part of Startorch Academy's Special Response Force. She works from the shadows, she's been an incredible help to us all.
If, in your fight with the Threnodian, you require aid...
...You may make a wish to me.
How about I go first? I wish for peace and safety across the Dimmr Plains.
Hehe, Hiyuki is the strong and silent type. Spend enough time with her, though, and you'll find she's rather fascinating.
Why didn't you introduce us before? Why the reveal now?
No point in hiding what's already in plain sight. Besides, you're both strong and it's safer if you work together.
What did she mean, "make a wish?"
Just what it sounds like. But… don't make one unless it's life or death.
Granting wishes... always comes with a price.
I've sent you intel on high-concentration Voidmatter zones. Review them when you can.
I couldn't say this with Chase present, but I think you understand. For him, this is a calculated investment. For you, it's a suicide mission.
You've been in Voidspace. You've faced it. If Aleph-1 truly lies beyond that Gate, It'll sense you the moment you cross over... This won't be easy.
The Exostrider isn't fully repaired. If something goes wrong, it may not be able to hold out against Aleph-1. And the Collective won't risk an extraction if a Voidmatter outbreak is imminent.
…Once you enter the Gate, you'll be completely alone. Are you ready, {PlayerName}?
Of course.
And just so you know...
I've been in situations like this more times than I can count.
If I only cared about walking away clean, I wouldn't have gotten this far, or be standing here now.
So… thanks for the concern, President. But I've been ready.
Heh... Very good. I'm relieved you've thought it through.
I'll be here with the others, monitoring the Voidmatter Barriers. If something happens, I have to respond immediately as the Academy's head.
We'll probably be able to maintain a data feed, but based on past intel the Voidmatter's "disintegration" effect will destabilize the signal.
It'll likely cut out once you reach the Keystone. From that point on, every call is yours.
I understand.
President Lucilla, we've checked her condition. No signs of Voidmatter contamination. She's stable.
Alright. Go on, Rabelle College freshman.
The students and faculty of Startorch Academy have been waiting for the day the Exostrider awakens. May you return victorious.
I remember you came across Aemeath's traces before.
Yes. Lucilla spoke of what that child did. She is a good one. Strong... But her strength does not change the fact that she is a student.
That we allow a child to bear such a burden is a failure on our part as adults.
I sent this white bird to search the Voidmatter's concentrated depths. And I am certain I felt her frequency.
Somewhere in Voidspace, she waits. The Collective pursues what is right, but your quest to save the one you hold dear? That, I understand.
Very good—! But I'm afraid we can't say more than that, okay? Right now, both sides want the same thing. That's the best we could ask for.

You reach the Dimmr Plains and find Hiyuki, the Ashinohara miko you met before. She offers you her origami familiar, a way to reach her if you are in need, then departs to track the Fractsidus.
Lucilla will stay behind with the other researchers to monitor the Voidmatter Barriers, while you make your preparations to head toward the Keystone's location alone.
Voidmatter Gauge readings are stable. No abnormal fluctuations at the Keystone.
{PlayerName}'s vitals are within the expected range… All readings normal…
Frequency... Bzzt... Delay... Bzzt...
(The signal's cut out. It's so quiet...)
(Are these... Soliskin?)
Chululu!
(Are these little guys guiding me to the Solvein Heartwood?)
Alright then, let's go together.
(The Soliskin merged into the roots.)
(They didn't return to the Exostrider through the Solistrees. They used themselves to reinforce the Keystone's seal...)
Aemeath!
Am I... back at the Academy?
You okay? Ugh, I feel terrible too...
Something just yeeted us outta the Threnodian's gut! Rapid interdimension whiplash is no stroll through the park, I tell ya...
The Exostrider isn't here.
The big guy's still stuck inside the Threnodian…
Did you see that freaky black hand that popped up in the cockpit? The whole dimension freaked out! Like it was tryna spit us out!
Next second, the Voidmatter had us flushed out here!
I saw Nivora in the flashes. It's the Fractsidus. Has to be.
Everyone was standing right here. This is where Voidmatter began to contaminate the sky.
And Aleph-1 was watching me and the Exostrider beyond the Gate...
Mentor! Finally got a hold of your signal!
...We lost contact for an entire day. Everyone's been worried.
(An entire day? Felt like minutes... Time distortion from being inside the black hole?)
Mornye, I'm fine. We had an incident. The Exostrider is still inside Voidspace beyond the Gate.
And now… even the Academy inside the Voidmater Barriers is getting hit by Void Storms. What happened while I was gone?
After you went in, the data transmission was perfect. We were celebrating.
But not long after, Void Storms began to erupt even inside the Academy. We haven't been able to find any breach or source to explain it.
Most of Lahai-Roi has been evacuated to the Collective's escape pods with Black Shores support.
Those who remain are gathered here at the Institute.
(Whose eyes was I seeing through in those flashes? If they're the cause of the Threnodian's breach, could they be a Harbinger? Like in Rinascita?)
(In that moment, that "shell"... She had the same look in her eyes as the real Aemeath.)
(Why was she so set on giving me this Cassette? What was she trying to say?)
(Looks like... I'll have to open it up to find out.)
I'm coming to you. I brought something back from the other side and want you to see it first.
The Threnodian struck like a bolt out of the blue. We may not know why yet, but we need to figure out our next move together.
Understood. We'll be waiting for you at the Institute.

After piloting the Exostrider through the Stridergate, an unknown force rips you from the cockpit. Just before you're thrown out of Voidspace, Aemeath's shell presses the Cassette into your hands. Fragments of memory flash through your mind—Nivora, smiling, reaching for you. The Academy, slowly consumed by Voidmatter. 
You wake back at the Academy. Mornye quickly informs you that Voidmatter erupted inside the Academy without warning and most personnel have already evacuated to escape pods.  And it's already been one full day since your last memory of entering the Stridergate.
(That's a lot of WavesLine messages.)
(All from when I was beyond the Stridergate... I really made them worry.)
The Soliskin have swept the area. None remain in Bjartr Woods.
Someone just posted a call for help on the campus's Wavesline group chat. I'll go back to run a final check later!
Sigrika, are you sure you alone can handle it?
Don't worry. I have my runes. They'll help me drive back any contaminated Exoswarm! The Soliskin will guide me too.
I'm coming with you. Finding people is one thing, hauling them back is another. It'll be faster if we take my motorbike.
Status report, Professor Mornye.
The launch orbit has been confirmed. The Drive Replica is projected to launch in 16 hours, at which point it will initiate Duskveil.
Then we evacuate every remaining soul in Lahai-Roi before that window closes.
Check the ships at the Roya Frostlands port, in addition to the escape pods.
The Exostrider...
I heard from Professor Mornye. You went dark for a full day. We thought we'd lost you to Voidspace.
Activating the Exostrider before it was fully self-regenerated was a massive gamble. We all knew the stakes.
We hoped for the best, and of course, we prepared for the worst.
You're alive. That alone is good news for all of us.
No time to kick yourself now. I've got a mountain of work waiting for you, {PlayerName}.
Please have a look at this, Mentor.
The Research Institute is running the final modifications on the Drive Replica now. The Roya are assisting with assembly and verification.
It's built from the framework of the fallen Reactor Drive. Various materials from the Exostrider, too.
It's… it's everything Lahai-Roi has been working toward. This "man-made" heart is our final legacy for Solaris.
In 16 hours, we launch it into the Canopy above Lahai-Roi.
We only finished it because of the data you sent back from beyond the Stridergate.
See? Not a total loss after all.
...Thank you. All of you.
About what I experienced beyond the Gate… there's something I want to show you.
I don't think she gave me this Cassette for no reason.
Whether it's her "shell" or Aemeath herself, she was trying to tell us something.
A Cassette retrieved from within the Threnodian… Heh. Now that is intriguing.
Professor Mornye, is the analyzer in the Cassette Depository still operational?
We can head there now.
Once evacuation is complete, it will activate, transforming the Canopy into Duskveil. It'll contain the spread of Voidmatter.
We don't know how long Duskveil will hold. No one stays a second longer than necessary.

You return to the Research Institute, where a small group of people chose to stay to assist with the evacuation. Those you know comfort you over the loss of the Exostrider, and reassure you that the Voidmatter data obtained through the operation will be instrumental to the Drive Replica's completion. Soon it will sustain "Duskveil," holding back the spread of Voidmatter.
You retrieve Aemeath's Cassette and share with everyone what you experienced beyond the Stridergate.
What's happening? Why are all the Cassettes rewinding…?
The whole server bank's going nuts! Every screen is flickering!
The data volume is far beyond the Cassette's capacity. It's as if thousands of people left their records in it...
The storage system has destabilized. It's now flooding data into every archived Cassette in the room.
But this isn't memory data.
Aside from the corrupted junk, the compressed mass consists of coordinates.
Coordinates? From beyond the Gate? Are they... locations inside the Threnodian's black hole?
Based on your report, Mentor, conventional spacetime doesn't apply in Voidspace. You can't plot a standard map there.
If Aemeath went to these lengths to give us this, she wouldn't have made it impossible to follow.
What if they're coordinates in Lahai-Roi?
...If such is the case, the origin might be... Please, allow me.
A uniform format, these coordinates share. I have verified one hundred and fifty points using Sundermere as the origin.
Each one aligns with a site of Royan runes etched upon stone. Just as you beheld within Solisylum.
Forged by the Exostrider, these patterns are. Void Storms do not affect them. We have only glimpsed fragments of their meaning through the lens of Solisylum.
I see. Let me overlay these coordinates onto a Lahai-Roi map. It should make things clearer for us.
Done. Using Sundermere as the origin, the distribution looks like this.
Everything matches. I'm spotting every Royan rune site in Bjartr Woods on this map.
(But what about the other coordinates...)
Wait, I know that spot. That's where the Void Storm hit at the start of all this.
You know... that time. When we first met.
The time we ran into a Voidworm?
The time you hit me with your motorbike?
Yeah, yeah, that one.
Ahaha, sorry... Ahem, anyway! Back to business!
Void Storms...
If we reverse-match the coordinates by transcription order…
Then Royan runes could be marking every spot a Void Storm has ever hit!
...So it is. We Roya have observed something similar, but never perceived it as a universal law.
Well, the runes aren't exactly neon signs on the stone boulders saying "LOOK HERE."
There are too many locations. With the personnel we have left and the autonomous bots...
Leave the manpower to me.
You want to bring people back from the escape pods?
Precisely, {PlayerName}.
With Void Storms spreading, sending people back out to find hidden runes is too dangerous.
Any venture into the unknown carries risk, {PlayerName}.
Studying the Threnodian was a risk. Playing chess with the Fractsidus was a risk.
And piloting the Exostrider through the Stridergate was certainly a risk.
But where there is a sliver of hope, the Spacetrek Collective will always find people willing to bet on it. Once Duskveil is initiated, this place becomes a sealed tomb.
The intelligence retrieved from Voidspace is our only remaining asset up for study. It's our only leverage against Aleph-1 before Duskveil fails.
We have the capability, the responsibility, and the will to secure this Voidspace data.
Face the unknown, and we may lose much. Flee from it, and we lose everything.
Then leave Bjartr Woods to me.
It's the most heavily contaminated area. If this is because of what happened to the Exostrider—
Capability, responsibility, and the will...
I have them all.
Then, then...Please give me your hand, {PlayerName}!
Use this. It will guide you to other Royan runes.
Oh, and take this one too! It's for protection. With it, you'll definitely come back safely!
With your runes, I'll be fine.
With Siggy's magical rune, nothing bad will happen!
The clock starts now. Recover all Royan runes before Duskveil gets initiated.

The moment the Cassette is analyzed, screens connected to the entire server bank inside the Depository begin to flicker. This Cassette holds thousands of records, far exceeding its capacity. Through Mornye and the Shaper's analysis, you discover that it holds a dense web of coordinates across Lahai-Roi, each one a site where Void Storms ravaged and Royan runes were found.
In the end, the need for answers outweighs the risk. Chase greenlights the urgent task of collecting these runes to confirm the Cassette's information before "Duskveil" is initiated.
Oh...
My hand feels so warm. Incredible, it's like magic...
Huh... so this is a Royan rune...
I'm splitting everyone into teams. They'll depart in waves according to their assigned groups.
Good. I will prepare the interpretation for the Royan runes.
I'll take charge... Yes, I'll make sure none of the Spacetrek Collective's proprietary tech leaks...
Preliminary deployment is complete. All teams must reach their assigned sectors on schedule.
The teacher team has reached the high-risk zone ahead of time. Beginning full sector scan now!
Student Council here. We've engaged a rampaging Exoswarm herd. Estimated arrival at the mid-risk zone in two minutes.
All medical units are deployed sector-wide. Standing by to ensure personnel safety.
This is the Command Post. We're positioned at the Void Storm's edge. All Void Storm trajectory updates will be sent to your Terminals in real time.
Remember this... We all come home.
What's your status? I saw you dash straight into the most contaminated area when I passed Bjartr Woods.
So far, so good. How's it looking on your end?
My section is pretty far out. On the bright side, they gave me a permit to ignore all speed limits!
Catch you on the other side!
Um... is this coming through? Sigrika reporting. I'm halfway finished with the rune deciphering!
Respected Shaper, are you alright?
All is well. No need for concern... This is a public channel...
Lucilla, how are things on your side?
We're almost there. Sigrika's taken some pressure off the Heliodic Six. Rune deciphering is going well.
Start heading back. Most teams are already returning.
...I'll be there soon.
According to the map, the first one should be around here.
Huh. Didn't notice before, but… there're some shredded frequencies hanging around this spot.
Abby? You're awake?
Didn't seem like the right time for a chat before! But hey, you've got a real knack for rallying the troops, huh? Hehe.
The rune's responding...
Whoa! Something's glowing over there!
Those outer rune fragments... Line them up just right, and they might connect with the runes on the boulders. Let's try.
Too slow. Need to activate the rune again.
Jackpot! We actually found one...!
Good. I'll send it to the Shaper and the others for a look.
All right. On to the next rune.
Same as before...
Ugh, we were so close! Let's try again!
One spot cleared...
Right, now I just need to contact President Lucilla...
{PlayerName}! What are you doin' here?
How's it going?
Just checking in on you.
Everything's been smooth on this end. Weren't many runes left to collect near the Academy.
Plus... you know. Guess my days in the New Fed weren't all for nothing. I'm just glad it's me out here instead of the other students.
Yeah... Still, better to play it safe. Don't stay out here longer than you need to.
Um, which student was it again who volunteered for the most dangerous zone? Now {Male=he's;Female=she's} telling ME to play it safe? Relax.
I got it. But our window is ticking by, so let's get back to our missions.
Time to head back...
{PlayerName}? Why are you at the Academy? Weren't you supposed to be in charge of the Bjartr Woods sector?
Just checking on the situation here.
Just checking in on you.
Things have been progressing smoothly. Lynae's working on it too.
Me? I'm fine, but the situation in Bjartr Woods is complicated. You need to be careful.
When I saw you return from the Stridergate, I wasn't sure if I should say something comforting... I thought you would—
I would be more depressed?
I would be more discouraged?
Don't worry. I don't have time for self-pity.
I'll leave things here to you, Chisa. It's time for me to get back to my own mission.
Right. See you later!
Get back to the Institute. Now!
Professor, I'm going stir-crazy in there. Let me do something!
You just got out of emergency debridement. You don't get a say.
If you've got energy to spare, stay and help carry the injured. You're not going anywhere near the danger zone again!
Professor! We just got word from President Lucilla, a Void Storm is about to hit up ahead!
Then we find another road.
There's no other road. Nothing drivable!
...Not a problem, we'll just make one ourselves. Bring me the expedition motorbike!
Miss Zahira! We recovered the rune from the area sealed off by the Void Storm!
If not for the route map you charted, we never would've found a path around it.
Zahira says nothing. A moment passed...
A route! I saw an image of a route we can take in my head!
Let's move out!
You have some gall to show your face here again.
I care not for Lahai-Roi. I have wanted to escape my entire life.
But... it is still my home. Here, we found this rune.
One deed does not wash away what you are. You are still a disgrace.
I'm going to the next site, Father.
That girl... still no response. And her name wasn't on the operation roster that came through the Archive either...
Nastasha, we need to move. Our window to move before the next Void Storm won't last.
Understood. I'll drop a quick message and join you.
You've looked uneasy since the evacuation began. What is it?
Just one kid I can't stop worrying about... She's smart. She'll manage, right?
Come on. We should go.
We get a five-minute window. No more. Three rune sites have to be secured in that time.
Senior...
No. Save it. I'm the director. I go first.
If Nastasha and I don't make it back, then once you're safe, bring our Cassettes home.
This angle gets everything in frame... Done.
How do you remember it so well? I hadn't even opened the map, and you were already dragging me over.
A long time ago, I was watching the Exoswarm and got distracted staring at these things. Lost the whole herd...
My mother nearly skinned me alive for that one... After that, I couldn't help being wary of this place.
Didn't know they were runes back then, obviously. But a memory like that sticks... Haha...
Then think again. Any other places you're "wary of"?
...What's that supposed to mean? That I'm always getting distracted?
But okay, I can think of a few other times...
Maja's team works insanely fast...
How are they pulling this off?
We're down to two motorbikes. The injured and the students go first. I'll head to the nearest team on foot.
And if a Void Storm crosses your path?
Exactly! Let me stay! I'm younger. Faster on my feet!
Absolutely not. I'm not saving myself by abandoning a student.
Don't worry, Hyvatia's data will help me chart a course around the Storm.
Have a little faith in my life's work!
Status on the students?
Don't worry, ma'am. They're trickling back in.
Keep the records updated. We are not leaving anyone unaccounted for.
Yes, ma'am!
One thing, though. Denia from the Department of Voidmatters hasn't answered us, and her name isn't on the roster of those who took shelter.
Denia, hm... She'll be fine. The President will have taken care of that.
Chisa is amazing. That giant TD didn't even last a second against her.
I figured she was just the brainy type. Didn't expect her to be that good in a fight too...
You think this operation might be our chance? To become friends with her?
Uh... maybe? Though she kind of never looks us in the eye...
Then we go to her first!
Well? Any signs of the runes?
Hold on. One more sweep... It should be somewhere close by...
Based on these coordinates, it should be right here in our search radius.
Then let's think differently. What if that big rune formation wasn't carved by a single Void Storm?
You mean it's multiple runes stacked together? Hurry and bring over a Roya student!
You had no business coming out of the shelter. You should've stayed there and waited for evacuation!
Professor, if playing it safe was all I wanted out of life, I never would've come to Lahai-Roi.
No one's questioning your nerve. I'm telling you not to throw your life away.
You've already done more than your fair share.
Not even close. Not in this situation!
Tove, Sigrika reached out to you too, right?
She did. From what she described, I know a few places that might have runes.
They're not easy to get to, but a Soliskin showed me the way before.
Marigold... Even now, it's still helping us.
The Lahai-Roi that child fought to protect will not be left to sink into eternal darkness.
President Lucilla really sent {Male=him;Female=her} to Bjartr Woods by {Male=himself;Female=herself}...?
{PlayerName}, the freshman?
More likely {Male=he;Female=she} volunteered.
You sound awfully sure, Professor.
Hah. Whatever else Lucilla may be, she is not a woman who sends students to die.
Besides, {PlayerName} does seem to have a taste for chasing after the kind of trouble people write songs about.
Gear check! Supply check! All set?!
All set!
Charge those bikes to full! We're taking off all limits to Boost mode today!
How many sets has Lynae turned in already?
This is her fifth? Maybe sixth? I've lost count.
That kind of speed is just ridiculous...
No kidding. She's already on her second set of tires!
Feed Hyvatia's real-time data into I.R.I.S. too.
We can, but that kind of load could overwhelm it.
Don't underestimate "her."
"She" is everyone's fellow classmate here at Startorch Academy.
If I just do this nice and quiet...
Do what exactly?
Wah—! {PlayerName}... I'm just unlocking a couple settings on the bike. The usually restricted ones, heh.
Some of the others came back hurt. I figured... if I could do a little more, maybe they'd face less danger.
Lynae...
Oh, please. You don't get to lecture me. You're in charge of the most dangerous zone, remember?
Anyway, I'm counting on you to keep this secret from the teachers. Kay {PlayerName}?
This should be the last spot on my side... Lemme double check.
{PlayerName}? What are you doing here? Everything good on your end?
Not bad.
Keeping pace with you, at least.
Heh, sounds like we're both making good progress. I'm done here too. Next stop is the final location.
After we gather all the runes... you're heading back beyond the Stridergate, right?
When I think about that, doing all this feels weird...
I know what I'm signing up for.
Knowing isn't nearly enough. You gotta sign up with the intention to come back!
Don't let this be our last meeting.
Upload complete. We can move to the next location.
{PlayerName}, any trouble on your end?
It's been smooth.
I was nearby anyway, so I came by to check on you.
My side is progressing well too. President Lucilla's Void Storm reports came at the right moment.
Mm... I'm alright. You're the one who needs to be careful from here on.
Some things... really have changed. Back then, whenever a Void Storm hit, all we could do was run.
You mean what happened in Honami?
Yes. But now we can forecast them, and our defense systems last much longer.
So we won't lose this time. Right?
Aleph-1 won't break us.
Then I'll see you later.
I should probably head back soon...
{PlayerName}, are you returning too?
There's still something left for me to do.
Then I'll go with you.
No, I've got this.
They need all the hands they can get at the Academy right now.
...Alright.
{PlayerName}, about you going back into Voidspace...
Some things have to be done.
I know... I really do. But still...
Chisa... when summer comes, let's go back to that cafe in Honami together. We can visit Namipon. It asked us to come back and tell it about the Academy, remember?
So that means...
It means I'm coming back.
There was a shortcut here the whole time? You just saved us!
It's nothing. I only remembered there might be a path leading this way.
You're too modest. You know the entire plains like the back of your hand. Do you get sent out on fieldwork a lot?
Not quite. It's more like I've spent a lot of time watching everyone else build these roads...
Huh? Well, either way, we've made up time. That should be a rune over there. Let's go!
Mm... Understood. Royan rune collection is proceeding as planned.
According to the mission brief, we can head back once we're done here.
Voidmatter concentrations are increasing. We can't stay here for long.
Okay, according to the markers, the location should be nearby...
Not sure if we can make it this time. If we do, then I'll—
Uh-uh, not this again. You'd have quit a long time ago if you actually meant it. Just hurry up and get the work done.
You had no business coming out of the shelter. You should've stayed there and waited for evacuation!
Professor, if playing it safe was all I wanted out of life, I never would've come to Lahai-Roi.
We're running out of time. Let's finish our part and see if anyone else needs help.
Mm. Honestly, we shouldn't have asked the students to take this risk with us...
Don't worry about them. They work much faster than we do.
Lucilla sent a single student to Bjartr Woods?! Has she lost her mind?
Voidmatter concentrations there are too high for us to handle. Even if we go now, we would just be dead weight. Have some faith in that student.
Where is that miko? Is she still down there?
Yeah. Otherwise, we would have been swarmed by those monsters by now.
Wait, what happened to your leg?
Sprained. Nothing serious. Did you send the rune data back?
Already did.
If anything happens to me, bring my Cassette back. The password to my safe is my work ID.
Cut the crap. We're going back! Together!
Well? Any sign of the runes?
Hold on. One more sweep... it should be somewhere close by.
Didn't expect these nonsensical runes to be this much help.
It's all thanks to that Roya girl.
Have you ever thought about... the future of Lahai-Roi?
Just do what we can for now. One step at a time. That way, we won't have any regrets when we're back in the shelter.
Easy there. It's likely just exhaustion. I'll get you both back to the Institute as soon as I can.
The runes...
Stay still. We've done our part. N.A.N.A. will get us back.
Good... that's... good...
Hey! Don't go quiet on me! Okay, forget staying still. Wake up! Wake up, damn it!
S.I.G.M.A., send the report to command. I'm counting on you.
Understood. Scanning area information... Comparison complete... Integrating data.
Didn't think the Voidmatter would leak up from underground. There's no one down there, right?
No idea. Command says the lower levels aren't our problem. Guess that miko's stuck dealing with it by herself again...
Allocating full processing power to conduct a unified screening of all historical data regarding the Spacetrek Collective and Startorch Academy.
Please vacate the current area and prepare for immediate evacuation.
And don't worry about me. I.R.I.S. has your back. Always have, always will.
(Aemeath once said Voidspace in the depths of the Threnodian's black hole is a higher dimension. Like a kaleidoscope.)
(Time and space here don't flow. They're tangled. Is all of this… inside Aleph-1?)
(Those figures... That's Spacetrek Collective and Royan clothing.)
(They're reaching for something. Can't make out what.)
(That frequency... Is it Aemeath?)
Made it inside.
Starting to feel... dizzy...
Voidspace... Even the second time, it gets under your skin.
...Is it trying to tell me something?
I will handle this.
It's fine.
It's that girl... Be cautious.
Her mind is fraying.
Urgh...
...She disappeared? That child will not last long out there.
This is the only way forward.
No... don't—
Is she stopping us of her own will?
Let me handle this.
Perhaps we try that path of light refracted from the ice?
Let's go. We're almost there.
That girl… What is your assessment?
If she's being forced to bear the Threnodian's power, then she needs our help.
No... You can't—! I pushed you out! Why did you come back?!
It's watching you! Aleph-1 is watching you!
...Don't go there... please... You can't!
Watch out.
(If I can get closer, I can use the power of Separation...)
WHY WON'T YOU JUST STOP?!
Sorry... I... You really shouldn't go...
{PlayerName}... I sent you away... So why...?
So it was you, Denia... the one who pushed me out of Voidspace.
I will deal with this...
Tch... wait for another opening.
My head... it hurts...
The time… I can't… hold it…!
No... please... don't... don't eat me...
Now. Do it.
Now, now. Playtime is over.
Aleph-1 seems to hold little interest in anything besides humans.
Most of the structures it devoured have drifted here.
Let me freeze those blocks. It will make them easier to break.
Denia! Can you hear me?!
We need to use these blocks to make the climb!
It will eat you...
Come back to me, sweet girl.
Still regretting that you didn't step into that shop and try that red bean pudding with the former miko, aren't you?
...What's your point?
The peaceful streets of Honami. The sweetness of red bean pudding. You know none of it is real.
The illusion didn't deceive you, Miko of Flaming Sakura. You were the one who deceived yourself.
Desperate to play pretend? Why not commit? Please, this performance is only beginning.
The letter is stiff with frost. The handwriting is neat and restrained, quiet on the page, like it never once hoped for an audience.
Flaming Sakura. Those delicate petals feed themselves to the fire, ashes scattering in the wind, until nothing remains but a dry, bitter aftertaste.
The letter is stiff with frost. The handwriting is neat and restrained, quiet on the page, like it never once hoped for an audience.
Lady Miko. I know you have never once opened a letter from the Fractsidus. Very well, let your own eyes bear witness instead.
A sheet of paper, pristine to a fault. The elegant handwriting dances across the page as if eager for someone to read it.
